title: Explainable 3D-CNN for Schizophrenia Detection using Multi-Site Structural MRI
authors: Sameen Raza, Amogh Gupta, Subhashree Mishra, Bhabani Shankar Prasad Mishra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Schizophrenia is a chronic neuropsychiatric disorder characterized by structural brain abnormalities detectable using structural MRI (sMRI) [3], [5], [6]. This study proposes an explainable three-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (3D-CNN) for automated schizophrenia classification using the multi-site COINSTAC MCIC VBM dataset [16], [31], comprising 3,729 subjects. The model learns volumetric representations directly from preprocessed 64 × 64 × 64 voxel inputs and the efficiency of the model is verified by calculating different parameters like accuracy, F1-score, Cohen’s Kappa and AUC. Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) [28] is integrated to localize discriminative brain regions and enhance clinical interpretability. Quantitative overlap analysis with anatomically defined regions of interest (ROIs) demonstrates substantial correspondence of activation within hippocampal and temporal regions, which are consistently implicated in schizophrenia [6], [7]. Training stability is validated through epoch-wise loss and accuracy curves, and computational feasibility is analyzed to support real-world clinical deployment. The proposed framework achieves competitive classification performance compared to recent 3D-CNN approaches [14], [27], [32], [33] while incorporating quantitative explainability on multi-site data. These findings highlight that volumetric deep learning combined with quantitative interpretability analysis provides a robust and clinically meaningful approach for schizophrenia classification.

title: Federated Cloud Intelligence: A Privacy-Preserving, Trustworthy and Sustainable Framework for Multi-Cloud AI
authors: Abuzar Khan, Ahmad Junaid, Abid Iqbal, Ghassan Husnain
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: This study proposes a Federated Cloud Intelligence for Privacy-Preserving AI, with new layered framework that can support secure and eco friendly learning across different cloud providers. Instead of centralizing data, our method trains models locally on varied client datasets and combines their updates using federated learning (FL) to stay compliant with data protection rules. The experiment have shown that the federated setup reached an average accuracy of 0.844 over five communication rounds, just slightly lower than the centralized baseline of 0.850. Meanwhile, the loss decreased from 0.367 to 0.285, coming close to the centralized value of 0.318. To build trust, a blockchain-based layer that permanently stored updates with little extra cost, adding blocks each round with an average consensus delay of 0.189 seconds. Tests showed that this consensus process reduced the impact of malicious client attacks, keeping accuracy stable around 0.827. Further it is then incorporated with zero-knowledge proofs (ZKM), where adds only 0.196 seconds of latency and 260–360 MB GPU memory overhead and showcases an accuracy up to 0.844. A reinforcement learning agent optimized workload scheduling by shifting the computation from AWS to GCP, reducing carbon scores by 20% with minimal accuracy trade-off. Finally, explainability analysis revealed balanced provider contributions from 0.021 to 0.023 and highlighted key features such as logPurchases and storePurchases.

title: Best Implementation Strategy of PS5000 in Smart TV Chip Testing
authors: Chelsea Zhou
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Smart TV chip testing requires robust solutions for complex functions such as eARC, MIPI, and analog performance verification. PS5000 plays a critical role in enabling digital high-speed tests, AC parameter checks, and high-resolution analog measurements. Traditional methods face limitations in stability and efficiency. To address these challenges, this work introduces an optimized implementation strategy leveraging PS5000’s advanced features, including CDR & FA for data integrity, enhanced TMU for precise timing analysis, and C-DAC for linearity testing. These improvements significantly enhance test accuracy, reduce time cost, and achieve up to 15% yield improvement in real-world applications.

title: AI–ML–Based IP Framework For Accurate Brain Tumor Detection in IoT-Enabled Medical Imaging
authors: Shwetha G.K., Sarika Ghanshyam Jadhav, Suruthi S., A. Shaji George, Athira Manoj, T.C. Manjunath
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: Malignant brain cancer is ultimate outcome of uncontrolled proliferation of brain cells, which in turn causes a host of other neurological disorders. A background survey is given w.r.t. significant obstacles to early diagnosis that can be overcome with use of Computer-Aided-Diagnosis system. One of these obstacles is the proper identification of various diseases in MRI images. To improve accuracy of (MRI) scans we use the method of malignancy concepts. Finally, image containing brain tumor is located in the quadrant with highest MSF. Present research compares the suggested architecture to existing models that are discussed in order to demonstrate its merit. MRI brain tumor detection accuracy results of an impressive 97% with a precision of 95%, sensitivity of 98%, a specificity of 96%, a F1-score with 97%. Their experiment & simulation results conclude that developed architecture is robust, leading to an improvement in detection accuracy of brain tumors in various locations.

title: AI-Based Front-End Optical Identification for 2D Emerging Chips
authors: Shengxian Ke, Ho Hon Lawrence So, Xiaonan Deng, Yunfei Liu, Tongge Zhang, Yihan Tang, Jie Feng, Chen Wang
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: We present an AI-based front-end optical identification pipeline for 2D emerging chips using superpixel patches from routine experiments and characterization. A dual-head ResNet-18 predicts one primary label and multiple attributes. We compare direct supervised training with self-supervised pretraining followed by finetuning. Primary classification exceeds 0.99 accuracy across models, while attribute recognition benefits more from pretraining. DINO achieves the best overall attribute performance, improving micro-F1 from 0.830 (control) to 0.856. The results show that self-supervised pretraining improves fine-grained recognition while reducing dependence on large-scale manual labeling.

title: HistDiff: Diffusion Models Empowered by Histogram Matching for Reducing Content Color Interference in Style Transfer
authors: Siying Liu, Zhenye Liu, Qiananna KongLang, Yingnan Ma, Anup Basu
abstract: Previous methods for arbitrary style transfer often suffer from content-specified color interference, leading to undesirable color preservation or artifacts. In this paper, we propose a novel diffusion-based method, HistDiff, designed to enhance style expressiveness without compromising the integrity of the content structure. Specifically, we introduce a controllable histogram matching module that allows fine-grained management of color distribution. We utilize a pretrained diffusion model and incorporate an attention-based mechanism for style injection, which enables precise and localized style adaptation. Experimental results demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art performance, significantly improving both content preservation and style fidelity across various benchmarks.

title: Recommender Systems for Reducing Software Fault Rates in Automotive Software Development
authors: Dimitrios Datsogiannis, Wolfram Hardt
publish: 2025 International Symposium on Computer Science and Educational Technology (ISCSET)
abstract: This paper presents the research results of using a recommendation system (RS) to evaluate automotive development processes from the initial design phases to the final software release. The model uses a dynamic questionnaire managed by a recommender system, which aims to detect deficiencies and faults during the development while increasing transparency among stakeholders. The question pool with 137 questions, consists of weighted questions, derived from key development metrics and serves as the basis for this evaluation. Questions are selected during each development cycle with Contextual Multi-Armed Bandit (CMAB) algorithms, allowing for minimal stakeholder effort while maximizing feedback relevance. The current study explores the system's performance across four behavioral change scenarios that simulate different rates of software fault occurrence. Three reinforcement learning algorithms, eGreedy, LinUCB, and Thompson Sampling, were applied and compared. The tuning of the algorithms is also part of the performance assessment. The results are assessed based on the defined performance indicators. The findings indicate that the system is not only scalable but also achieves improved performance over time as it continues to operate and adapt to changing development conditions.

title: Advancing Crop Health: An Integrated Deep Learning Approach For Plant Disease Detection
authors: Murthuja P, Radha Krishna Ghodke, Mohan Reddy Pasurla, Prasanthi Kaluri
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Plant diseases present a significant threat to agricultural activities and food security particularly where experts cannot be easily put in place to detect the diseased plants. The proposed paper introduces an entire plant disease diagnosis system based on the use of deep learning to perform classification, lesion segmentation, severity Estimaton, and automated treatment prescription, which is useful on large commercial farms. The pipeline starts with a strong preprocessing step such as image normalization, image augmentation and noise removal to increase the performance in real world conditions. The main classifier is an EfficientNet-B4 model that has been fine-tuned on the PlantVillage dataset with 54,304 images of 38 plant classes and it has an 94 percent accuracy. Image processing methods detect infected regions and create lesion masks after the process of classification. The severity of the disease is computed as a percent of the lesion pixels of the total leaf pixels. The rule-based recommendation engine correlates each disease and level of severity with suitable biological, chemical and preventive interventions. The last interface shows the disease name, percentage severity and advice on the cure in a concise and easy to understand form that will allow agriculture decision support to be scaled and achieved in a practical manner.

title: Towards Responsible Conversational AI for Mental Health Support: Balancing Empathy, Fairness, and Safety with Deep Learning
authors: Fatima Khurshid, Akhtar Jamil
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Empathetic conversational AI systems have a lot of promise for supporting mental health therapy, even if their practical use can be constrained by high processing needs and persistent problems with safety, fairness, and interpretability. This work presents a lightweight, computationally efficient architecture for empathic dialogue analysis and response generation that combines emotion classification, zero-shot personality approximation, semantic answer retrieval, explicit safety filtering, and fairness auditing into a single pipeline. Sentence-BERT embeddings are used as a common semantic representation, which eliminates the need for large-scale generative models and allows for accurate empathy categorization and interpretable personality inference. Similarity-based retrieval produces empathetic replies with minimal computing overhead and emotional alignment. The framework includes a dedicated safety layer to exclude risky, catastrophic, and clinically inadequate outputs as well as a template-driven fairness audit to evaluate demographic robustness in order to facilitate responsible deployment. Strong empathy classification performance, high emotion consistency in retrieved replies, regulated safety behavior, and consistent empathetic outputs despite demographic fluctuations under computational limitations are all demonstrated by experimental evaluations carried out on a large-scale empathetic discussion dataset. These findings show that conversational AI with empathy and responsibility may be developed without requiring enormous amounts of processing power, providing a useful and morally sound basis for scalable mental health support systems.

title: A Secure Image Sharing System Using Hybrid Chaos-Based Encryption
authors: Molika Y, Puli Venkata Sai Yashwanth, P Vishnu Vardhan, Ravi Bhargav, Priyanka Singh
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Due to the nature of digital communications, there is a necessity for transmitting images through public networks in a secured manner. The proposed technique involves the use of a chaotic image encryption algorithm by dividing an image into smaller fragments then performing encryption on each fragment by means of Tent-Sine chaotic system. Encryption is achieved through permutation, substitution and XOR diffusion algorithms whereas SHA-256 algorithm will be employed in generating seeds for encryption.The proposed algorithm also involves the application of microservices in a sender-receiver authorizing process using the technologies Node.js, MongoDB and Python Flask. From the experimental analysis carried out, the following results were found regarding security performance. NPCR of more than 99.5%, acceptable UACI values, entropy of more than 7.999 and encryption time below 0.5 seconds for 256×256 images. The above indicates high security with regard to encryption and decryption processes.

title: Detection and Mitigation of DDoS Attacks in SDN Using DNN Approach
authors: Samantha S, Akshaya Shree C, Ishwarya R, Gowthami N, Tamilselvi S
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attack is one of the most significant security threats to SDN-based networks. In a DDoS attack, a large number of compromised devices, often part of a botnet, forward an overwhelming amount of traffic simultaneously to a targeted network or a service. The purpose is to exhaust resources and make the target unavailable to legitimate users, disrupting normal operations and causing service downtime. In context of Software Defined Networking, the network is prone to serious vulnerabilities because of its highly centralized architecture. Attackers can exploit this vulnerability by generating high volumes of traffic by flooding the SDN controller with an excessive number of TCP/UDP/ICMP requests and eventually slowing down the network’s response. An effective DNN-based DDoS detection and mitigation framework is needed to overcome this problem. Therefore, by utilizing Autoencoder-based Deep Learning approach and Harris Hawk Optimization (HHO), the proposed model is proven to perform well in dynamic network traffic scenarios and achieved an accuracy of 96.86% demonstrating its effectiveness in maintaining network reliability and availability.

title: A Study on Spatial Correlation Between Gas Pressure Difference and Film Thickness Based on an Unsupervised Learning Model
authors: Qiaoling Tian, Ziyang Liu, Guangyao Zhang, Kai Zhang
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: The uniformity of film thickness (THK) has a key impact on device performance in the plasma-enhanced chemical vapor deposition (PECVD) process. Traditional single-point analysis and other existing methods cannot effectively reveal whether there is a spatial correlation between pressure difference and film THK. They also fail to explain the complex spatial coupling mechanism. To address these problems, this paper proposes a deep convolutional autoencoder (CAE) method that integrates unsupervised learning ideas. The model takes the SH pressure distribution matrix inside the chamber as input. By using the advantage of convolutional neural networks in spatial feature extraction, it can automatically capture and quantify the spatial correlation between the gas pressure and film THK. Furthermore, our model can predict the film THK distribution from the pressure distribution. This provides a new theoretical basis and a data-driven approach for improving film THK uniformity.

title: Chemical Bonding at the Graphene Films/Cu Substrate Interface for High-Efficiency Heat Dissipation
authors: Chenfei Zhou, Xiuzhen Lu
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Escalating thermal management demands from increasing heat accumulation in high power density electronics under integration and miniaturization trends drive this chemically bonded graphene films/Cu substrate structure investigation. Through controlled functionalization, oxygen-containing functional groups were introduced on graphene films while polydopamine were engineered on Cu substrates, establishing chemical bonds at the interface. Thermal testing revealed superior performance of the engineered interface, while exhibiting amplified heat dissipation efficiency at elevated power densities. These results validate the effectiveness of chemical bonds in enhancing interfacial heat transport while highlighting the critical role of interface engineering in next-generation thermal management systems for high power density electronics.

title: Enhancing Trust in Maternal Health Predictions Through Explainable AI with Quantitative Assessment
authors: Maria Muzaffar, Ihtesham Ul Islam, Muhammad Sohail, Maemoona Farooq, Tauseef Rana
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Traditional black-box machine learning systems, are powerful, often fail to gain trust of clinical staff due to their opacity. For the solution of this gap, this research proposes an Explainable AI (XAI) framework designed to enhance trust and usability of ML models in maternal healthcare. Specifically, SHAP (SHapley Additive Explanations) and LIME (Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations) are employed to provide transparent, interpretable insights into model behavior. The study follows an empirical, model-based, and quantitative research design using two public and anonymized datasets: a multi-class Maternal Health Risk Prediction dataset and a binary Gestational Diabetes Mellitus (GDM) dataset. Preprocessing includes missing value imputation, IQR-based outlier removal, class balancing using SMOTE, and feature selection using Mutual Information and RFECV. Multiple models of machine learning i.e., Support Vector Machine (SVM), XGBoost, Neural Network, Random Forest, Decision Tree, and Logistic Regression, are evaluated using standard performance metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, specificity, F1-score, and AUC. Experimental results show that SVM achieves the highest accuracy 98.42% on the GDM dataset, while XGBoost leads on the maternal health dataset with 86.33% accuracy. The explainability of the models is evaluated using fidelity, consistency, robustness, and simplicity. The scores of these evaluation metrics for both datasets are fidelity (1.0 and 1.0), consistency (1.0 and 1.0), robustness (1.0 and 0.99) whereas, simplicity shows separate values for each features of both datasets. All these scores are optimal or near- optimal levels, confirmed the reliability of the generated explanations.

title: Machine-Learning-Based Surrogate Model for Predicting Plasma Etching Profiles
authors: ZiyaoLuo, Xiuzhen Lu
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Precise prediction of plasma etching profiles is required in advanced semiconductor manufacturing, where process development has traditionally been constrained by costly trial-and-error procedures and expensive physics-based simulations. In this study, a data-driven surrogate model based on deep neural networks (DNNs) is constructed to predict etched-profile geometries under varying process conditions. Multiple network architectures, regularization strategies, and preprocessing schemes are systematically evaluated through k-fold cross-validation. High prediction accuracy (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> > 0.98) is achieved, demonstrating that reliable surrogate modeling of plasma etching can be obtained at significantly reduced computational cost. The proposed framework provides a rapid alternative to high-fidelity simulations and establishes a foundation upon which future optimization or inverse-design methods may be developed.

title: Utilizing Blockchain and IPFS for Government Correspondences in Saudi Arabia
authors: Mohammed O. Alannsary
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: This paper proposes a permissioned blockchain and private IPFS framework aimed at enhancing inter-agency correspondence in Saudi Arabia. The proposed design supersedes the outdated centralized system, ensuring immutability, verifiability, and secure document exchange through the Government Secure Network. A pilot roadmap outlines governance, integration, and key performance indicators for nationwide implementation. The work presents an implementation-ready architecture bridging technical and policy dimensions of e-government modernization.

title: Ultra-Underactuated Coupled-Two-Wheeled Robot for Agile Omnidirectional Mobility
authors: Zhiguo Lu, Yiheng Zhao, Wanting Kong, Chong Liu, Yu Wang, Guoshuai Liu, Yulong Ren
abstract: Omnidirectional mobility in wheeled robots has conventionally required at least three actuators, limiting simplicity and efficiency. This article presents UcoBot, to the best of our knowledge, the first two-wheeled robot that achieves dynamic self-balancing and full omnidirectional motion with only two motors. The innovation lies in an ultra-underactuated, mechanically coupled design, where a four-bar linkage enables control of four degrees of freedom—pitch, longitudinal motion, yaw, and lateral displacement—through two conventional wheels. This minimalist architecture reduces mechanical complexity while enabling fast, agile navigation in confined spaces. Mathematical modeling of the system is performed, its controllability and convergence are analyzed, and an effective hierarchical decoupling cooperative control (HDCC) framework is developed. Simulations and physical experiments demonstrate high-precision trajectory tracking on square, circular, and S-shaped paths, with robust performance in narrow corridors and uneven outdoor terrains. By overcoming the traditional actuation bottleneck, UcoBot provides a novel minimalist architecture for highly maneuverable robots, enabling promising applications in service and logistics scenarios.

title: Reform of the Health Informatics Curriculum in Mongolia: Needs and Development Perspectives
authors: Purevdolgor Luvsantseren, Ajnai Luvsan-Ish, Javzmaa Tsend, Oyuntsetseg Sandag, Baasandorj Chilkhaasuren, Odgerel Boldbaatar, Akhit Tilubai, Nyamdavaa Uugandavaa, Darambazar Gantulga
publish: 2025 International Symposium on Computer Science and Educational Technology (ISCSET)
abstract: This To reform the Bachelor of Health Informatics curriculum in Mongolia, a comparative analysis was conducted with similar international programs, and the revised curriculum was developed based on satisfaction surveys from both graduates and employers. A satisfaction survey was conducted in which 49 graduates evaluated the curriculum using a 19-question questionnaire, and 14 employers assessed the graduates' competencies through an 8-question questionnaire, forming the basis for two distinct datasets. Factor analysis (PCA and EFA with Varimax rotation) was performed on the resulting dataset. The analysis identified five latent factors from the graduates' evaluations—Quality of Instruction, Practical Training and Skills, Learning Environment and Resources, Curriculum Structure, and Professional Development Opportunities–and one factor from the employers' assessments: Job Requirements and Skills Evaluation. Based on these findings, the revised curriculum was developed through a comparative analysis with programs from the University of Sydney, Oregon Tech, and Montana Tech. The updated curriculum consists of 120 credit hours and complies with Order A/147 of the Minister of Education and Science, as well as the IT Curriculum Guide 2017. A notable feature of the revised program is its emphasis on specialization in health data processing, information systems architecture, and computer networking. This research contributes to enhancing the quality of Health Informatics education and aligning the academic training with the needs of graduates and labor market demands.

title: Predicting Financial Success Through ESG Metrics: Leveraging Machine Learning and Explainable AI
authors: Abuzar Khan, Ahmad Junaid, Abid Iqbal, Ghassan Husnain
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: This study examined the relationship between Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) performance and financial outcomes via unified combination of machine learning and deep learning frameworks. A global and publicly available dataset of firms was analyzed by combining ESG indicators with financial metrics such as revenue, profit margin and market capitalization. Machine learning models such as XGBoost, LightGBM and TabNet, a state of the art are applied for both regression and classification analysis throughout the dataset. The results have shown strong predictive performance with classification accuracy reaching 0.88 and AUC values above 0.90 while the regression models has achieve R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> value above than 0.90 and RMSE as low as 0.34. Explainable AI methods such as SHAP and LIME have identified the market capitalization, water usage and carbon emissions as the most influential key drivers in the domain while governance remaining the leading ESG factor across most settings, while cross domain evaluation have further confirmed robustness with domain level accuracies of 0.69 and 0.78. Scenario simulations also indicated that targeted ESG improvements such as a 10% increase in governance scores produced measurable although modest gains in profitability. These findings were then incorporated into a prototype decision support system which allowed managers to test ESG strategies in an interactive way. Overall the framework delivers robust interpretable and practical insights that align sustainability practices with financial performance.

title: Bayesian Learning for Structure and Recipe Optimization with Limited Data and Mixed Variables
authors: Kai Zhang, Jiaxin Zhu, Guangyao Zhang
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Structural and recipe optimization is critical for achieving target process performance in semiconductor manufacturing, yet it is often constrained by limited experimental data and the presence of heterogeneous input variables in both continuous and discrete domains. This paper proposes a Bayesian learning framework based on Gaussian Process Regression for data-efficient modeling and optimization in mixed-variable spaces. The framework jointly accounts for continuous factors such as structural geometry, power, and gas flow, together with discrete settings such as recipe type and working conditions, enabling accurate mapping from equipment design and process parameters to key metrics including deposition rate and film uniformity. This probabilistic framework provides not only predictions but also uncertainty quantification under small-sample conditions. Case studies on semiconductor equipment design demonstrate that the approach enables efficient and effective co-optimization of structure and recipe, offering a practical path toward accelerating process development with limited data.

title: TASH-Net: A Novel Deep CNN for Brain Tumor Classification from MRI Images
authors: Hussein Alkatout, Jinan Charafeddine, Mohammad Attique Khan, Jihad Al Jaam, Taha Houda
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: The process of identifying brain tumors from MRI scans proves difficult because of the extensive data quantity and the intricate nature of tumors. The research introduces TASH as a hybrid deep convolutional neural network which combines Vision Transformer elements with MBConv blocks and fractal design structure in a 357-layer system that uses 6.7M parameters. TASH is compared with state-of-the-art pre-trained models including InceptionV3, ConvNeXt-Base, MNASNet, Vision Transformer, and Swin Transformer. All models were fine-tuned and evaluated on two public MRI datasets. The TASH model achieved 97% accuracy on Dataset 1 but InceptionV3 produced the best results (99%) when working with Dataset 2. The proposed hybrid architecture shows better classification results because it uses its parameters effectively which makes it appropriate for using MRI images to identify brain tumors.

title: Quantum-Inspired Hybrid Tamper Detection System for Secure Data Vaults in Offline Environments
authors: Prerna J, Puchha Bhavya, Ranjni U. A
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Conventional secure monitoring systems often rely on network connectivity and centralized servers. But this has been a very critical challenge in maintaining confidentiality and integrity in remote or offline regions where we can deploy secure data walls. This paper proposes the quantum-inspired hybrid tamper detection system that will integrate our software-simulated by PUF for cryptography, which generates a key and then a hardware-assisted vault monitor mechanism which will use a low-power sensor and third a secure encrypted tamper logging framework which is capable of detecting physical intrusion or any attempts without depending on the cloud. The system employs a quantum-inspired PUF model to derive unique and device-specific keys, improving resistance to cloning compared to traditional stored-key systems. Furthermore, a hybrid hardware architecture that integrates an infrared break-beam module, a photo-diode sensor, magnetic reed switches, and an ambient-light-resistant enclosure is designed to provide uninterrupted vault monitoring during low-illumination or nighttime conditions. All events are stored locally in an AES-GCM encrypted, hash-chained, append-only log that operates entirely offline. Experimental evaluation carried out through the implemented Python simulation demonstrates reliable key reconstruction and low-overhead event signing, while enabling reliably detection of log-tampering attempts under the tested conditions. The proposed system is low-cost, scalable, and well-suited for deployment in military, banking, and institutional environments where network connectivity is limited or restricted.

title: Further performance testing of p-type transistor with dual-curvature cavity
authors: Ying Zhou, Guanqun Yu, Jianlei Zhao, Hongjian Mu, Yingna Han, Baohua Shi
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: This work demonstrates the performance benefits of a dual-curvature cavity design in p-type transistor, building on prior reported studies while incorporating comprehensive electrical characterization. Fabricated in a mature semiconductor wafer production line, the dual-curvature cavity enables larger SiGe Source/Drain volume. Compared to the baseline ball-shaped cavity, the new design shows no obvious change in Vt roll-off and slightly improved DC performance. Additionally, ring oscillator tests reveal AC performance gains, validating the cavity’s advantage for both static and dynamic operation. Furthermore, this kind of cavity architecture exhibits excellent reliability with no observable degradation in HCI and TDDB characteristics. These results significantly extend prior literature by systematically analyzing and providing quantitative comparisons with baseline data based on multiple electrical and reliability parameters, offering a promising solution for advanced MOSFET scaling.

title: Cryo-CMOS Integrator-Based DAC for Scalable Biasing of Semiconductor Qubits
authors: Luc Enthoven, Job van Staveren, Jiang Gong, Masoud Babaie, Fabio Sebastiano
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: Semiconductor-based cryogenic quantum processors require the accurate biasing of a large number of gate electrodes, which are typically individually wired to room-temperature DACs. To prevent the wiring bottleneck when scaling to future very large processors, this work proposes a scalable cryo-CMOS DAC that can operate with a S&H demultiplexer close to the quantum processor. By adopting an integrator-based switched-capacitor DAC with a dynamically-biased high-voltage output stage, both the required large output range and high resolution can be achieved while sharing the DAC over a large number of electrodes, thus improving the power efficiency. Fabricated in a 22-nm FinFET technology, the DAC occupies 0.076 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. At 4.2 K (RT), it achieves an LSB of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$57.1~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>V (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$68.3~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>V) over a 3 V range with <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$188~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rms</sub> (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$192~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rms</sub>) noise and 36.5-LSB (12.6-LSB) INL while dissipating <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$157~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>W (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$138~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>W). The low power dissipation and the potential to drive more than 30,000 electrodes paves the way for scalable biasing of quantum processors operating down to mK temperatures.

title: Intelligent Bearing Fault Detection: Deep Learning Model Assessment and Embedded System Deployment
authors: Andres Felipe Cotrino Herrera, Jesús Alfonso López Sotelo, Alonso Toro Lazo, Juan Carlos Blandón Andrade
abstract: Bearings are vital parts used in many industrial settings; however, their failures can greatly reduce system efficiency and operational reliability. In this context, AI-driven predictive maintenance is an effective approach for identifying and classifying bearing faults via vibration analysis. This study creates a custom dataset by recording vibration signals from a DC motor operating under various fault conditions (bearing without lubrication, bearing with one missing ball, and bearing with two missing balls), as well as under normal operation (bearing without failure), using an accelerometer and a controlled test bench. Furthermore, four neural network models Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Transformer were trained and evaluated based on accuracy, recall, and F1-score. The CNN model performed best, achieving a 99.95% accuracy on the validation dataset. This model was then implemented on an ESP32, reaching 94.2% accuracy during real-time testing. These results demonstrate that AI-based fault detection systems can be effectively deployed on resource-limited platforms, providing a promising solution for predictive maintenance and educational efforts to boost STEM skills.

title: Personalized Healthcare Recommendations for Diabetic Patients using Knowledge Graph Link Prediction
authors: Nasrullah Khan, Zubair Shah
abstract: Effective diabetes risk management requires individualized, data-driven healthcare support. The deeper semantic connections between medical entities are often overlooked by conventional recommender systems, which lowers the quality of insights they can offer to specific patients. In this paper, a healthcare knowledge graph (HKG)-based framework for knowledge graph link prediction-based personalized healthcare recommendations (PHR) is introduced. The framework creates a single, cohesive HKG by combining unstructured user opinions with structured medical data, enhanced by emotional cues and new disease trends. By using a margin-based ranking loss during training, graph embedding models (which are based on GNN encoders) are able to acquire significant semantic representations of both entities and relations. Recommendations for suitable diets, medications, and lifestyle modifications are made after the predicted links are ranked according to their plausibility, clinical relevance, and the patient’s unique profile. The results of the experiments demonstrate that the suggested model effectively finds hidden associations, resulting in PHR for diabetic patients that is easier to understand. The source code and implementation details of this work are publicly available at https://github.com/dr-n-khan/PHR-KGLP.

title: Evaluating the Techno-Economic Viability of a Solar PV-Wind Turbine Hybrid System with Battery Storage for an Electric Vehicle Charging Station in Khobar, Saudi Arabia
authors: Ahmed S. AbdElrazek, Mohamed Soliman, Muhammad Khalid
abstract: The main aim of this investigation is to replicate and enhance a sustainable hybrid energy structure that combines solar photovoltaic, wind turbines, battery storage. The study employs the Homer simulation model to evaluate the scaling, cost, and control strategy of this hybrid power system. This work primarily focuses on determining the most efficient design for a renewable energy generation system architecture for a significant electric vehicle charging station. The hybrid power system is designed to meet an AC base load of 2424.25 kWh/day with peak consumption of 444 kW. The simulation results indicate that the optimized components and the cost of energy are at an optimal level and the optimal design in terms of renewable energy penetration.

title: Tool and Agent Selection for Large Language Model Agents in Production: A Survey
authors: Elias Lumer, Anmol Gulati, Faheem Nizar, Dzmitry Hedroits, Atharva Mehta, Henry Hwangbo, Vamse Kumar Subbiah, Pradeep Honaganahalli Basavaraju, James A. Burke
abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) agents have demonstrated remarkable abilities to interact with external tools, functions, Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, and agents. Due to the fast-paced nature of the industry, existing literature does not accurately represent the current state of tool and agent selection. This work provides the first detailed examination of tool selection from a production perspective, distinguishing between the frontend layer where users interact with agents through buttons, slash commands, or natural language, and the backend layer where retrieval, execution, orchestration, and memory enable scalable reasoning. The paper contributes a unified taxonomy of modern tool and agent selection approaches, a production-oriented framework covering dynamic tool retrieval, context engineering, execution methods, memory, and evaluation, and identifies challenges and promising avenues for research.

title: Ensuring Consistency of Large Language Model Outputs for Similar Prompts using Group Relative Policy Optimization
authors: Sonal Prabhune, Balaji Padmanabhan, Kaushik Dutta
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in business-critical domains such as finance, education, healthcare, and customer support, where users expect consistent and reliable recommendations. Yet LLMs often exhibit variability when prompts are phrased with minor differences, even when semantically equivalent. Such inconsistency undermines trust, complicates compliance, and disrupts user experience. While personalization is desirable in certain contexts, many enterprise scenarios, such as HR onboarding, customer support, or policy disclosure, require invariant information delivery regardless of phrasing or prior conversational history. Existing approaches, including retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and temperature tuning, improve factuality or reduce stochasticity, but cannot guarantee stability across equivalent prompts. In this paper, we propose a reinforcement learning framework based on Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to directly optimize for consistency. Unlike prior applications of GRPO, which have been limited to reasoning and code generation, we adapt GRPO to enforce the stability of information content across groups of semantically equivalent prompts. We introduce entropy-based helpfulness and stability rewards, treating prompt variants as groups and resetting conversational context to isolate phrasing effects. Experiments on investment and job recommendation tasks show that our GRPO-fine-tuned model reduces variability compared to the baseline LLM model. To our knowledge, this is a novel application of GRPO for aligning LLMs toward information consistency, reframing variability not as an acceptable feature of generative diversity, but as a correctable flaw in enterprise deployments.

title: Integrating Symbolic Tokenization, k-mers, and Vision Transformers for Molecular Representation in BloodBrain Barrier Prediction
authors: Hannah I. Da S. Marques, Giovanna A. P. Soares, Matheus Dalmolin, Raquel De M. Barbosa, Marcelo A. C. Fernandes
abstract: The prediction of blood–brain barrier (BBB) permeability is a central task in molecular property modeling due to the selective nature of this physiological interface. This work investigates a representation pipeline that integrates symbolic tokenization of SMILES sequences, k-mer co-occurrence matrices, and image-based encodings processed by a Vision Transformer (ViT). Each molecule is converted into a Molecule Image Representation (MIR), from which embedding vectors are extracted and used as input features for four supervised classifiers: Support Vector Machine (SVM), Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGB), Random Forest (RF), and Logistic Regression (LR). The evaluation is conducted on the BBBP and B3DB datasets under a reproducible experimental protocol that includes fixed random seeds, standardized data partitions, and repeated executions. Across both datasets, SVM and XGB obtain the largest AUC values in the validation and test sets, while RF and LR produce lower values under the same conditions. The results indicate that the MIR–ViT pipeline produces embedding vectors that support discrimination between BBB+ and BBB− samples and that nonlinear classifiers achieve the highest performance within this framework.

title: CHORD: A Novel XAI Framework for Guiding Counterfactuals via Higher-Order Sensitivity Analysis
authors: Zohaib Shahid, Yogachandran Rahulamathavan, Safak Dogan
abstract: Building trustworthy AI systems requires robust and mechanically transparent explainability methods. Although counterfactual explanations effectively provide ‘what-if’ scenarios, their inability to reveal the underlying decision mechanisms in neural networks often limits their reliability. This paper introduces Counterfactuals via Higher-Order Relational Derivatives (CHORD), a novel framework that bridges the gap between sensitivity analysis and counterfactual generation. Unlike traditional first-order methods, CHORD utilizes higher-order partial derivatives to explicitly quantify feature interactions, mapping the complex non-linear dependencies that drive the outputs of a neural network. We demonstrate on the UCI Adult Income and UCI Bank Marketing datasets how this interaction-aware analysis can guide the generation of counterfactuals by targeting groups of coupled features, rather than isolated inputs. It was observed that using CHORD, if the value of the most impactful feature (based on the highest second-order partial derivative) is increased, a lower value of the most impactful feature (from the first-order sensitivity analysis, based on partial derivatives) is required to change the output. Crucially, our experiments show that prioritizing these interactions moves beyond simply finding a valid recourse to understanding the structural mechanisms that enable the switching of predictions. This synthesis results in explanations that are actionable, mechanistically interpretable, and mathematically grounded in the curvature of the network, thereby significantly enhancing AI transparency.

title: Adaptation and Evaluation of EEG Classifiers for Regression-based Hand Trajectory Decoding
authors: Miho Ohsaki, Minoru Inoue, Kimiaki Shirahama
abstract: One of the common techniques for Brain Computer Interface (BCI) is Electroencephalography (EEG). EEG is mainly used for classifying body parts or motor movements imagined by the brain. There are not many attempts to decode hand trajectories using EEG based on regression, since EEG signals are noisy due to their noninvasiveness. However, if such regression-based decoding is achieved using EEG, it could contribute to assisting physically disabled people in their daily lives with simple noninvasive devices. Toward this ultimate goal, our present study aims to adapt and evaluate recent representative EEG classifiers for regression-based hand trajectory decoding. We adapt EEG classifiers, EEG-TCNet, EEG-Conformer, and EEG-TCNTransformer from classification to regression. Specifically, we enable them to predict the 3D trajectories of a thumb and index finger given EEG time series as input. We then empirically evaluate them and the baseline CNN-LSTM, using the WAY-EEG-GAL dataset. As an experimental result, the mean squared error (smaller is better), the coefficient of determination (larger is better), and the correlation coefficient (larger is better) were obtained as follows: 0.653, 0.346, and 0.640 for CNN-LSTM. 0.500, 0.499, and 0.715 for EEG-TCNet. 0.810, 0.191, and 0.445 for EEG-Conformer. 0.635, 0.360, and 0.618 for EEG-TCNTransformer. EEG-TCNet substantially outperformed the other models across all evaluation metrics, whereas EEG-Conformer and EEG-TCNTransformer did not surpass the baseline CNN-LSTM. This present regression result, which was obtained under limited data and conditions, differed from the past classification result. Its generality should be carefully pursued using more data and optimized conditions in future work.

title: State-Learning of Time Series Data with Contrastive Learning
authors: Phillip Johann Overlöper, Alexander Diedrich, Oliver Niggemann
abstract: This study improves the interpretability of multivariate time series by learning human-understandable sequences of discrete states in an unsupervised manner. We propose a contrastive regularisation framework for discrete state-learning and instantiate it with SOM-VAE and CatVAE. Our approach embeds the state-learning model in a Siamese setup and optimises its objective together with an NT-Xent contrastive term, encouraging consistent representations and state assignments under realistic perturbations. Experiments on three human activity recognition benchmarks (UCI HAR, MHEALTH, PAMAP2) show consistent improvements over the corresponding base models in clustering quality (Purity, NMI) and entropy-based interpretability metrics, indicating more label-coherent and non-degenerate state representations.

title: A Feature of the Three-Phase Active Filter Power Spectrum in the Reactive Current Generation Mode
authors: Artur Postil, Oleksii Bialobrzheskyi, Maxim Oliynichenko
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Energy Smart Systems (ESS)
abstract: Power active filters have become widespread as a tool for compensating power quality disturbances caused by non-linear and asymmetric loads. Based on the analysis of known studies of similar devices, it was established that the authors widely use the Fourier series expansion of the instantaneous power of an alternating current circuit. At the same time, the issues of energy redistribution in the elements of the power circuit have not been investigated. On the basis of the scheme of replacing the power circuit of the device, spectral modeling of the case of sinusoidal current of the network circuit was performed. It was established that the network phase power at the fundamental frequency is the sum of the inductance power and output power of the converter, and at the modulation frequency it is the difference, while the amplitude of the modulation components of the inductance and converter power is comparable to the amplitude of the main harmonic of the network power. The specified features should be taken into account when choosing the inductive elements of the power active filter.

title: Technical and Economic Indicators of the Energy Node, Which Supports the Average Daily WPP Generating Power
authors: Viktor Denysov, Tetiana Nechaieva
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Energy Smart Systems (ESS)
abstract: An approach to modeling the joint operation of wind power plant with a built-in storage system is proposed, which ensures the maintenance of the average daily power supply capacity to the grid throughout the day. If it is impossible to provide the declared capacity at the expense of the wind power plant and accumulated electricity capacity, the reserve highly maneuverable capacity used. In the event of an excess of the total generating capacity of the power node, the capacity of the supplied to the grid electricity limited. Calculations of the hourly aggregate wind power plant capacity for the integrated power system of Ukraine for 365 days of 2019 have been carried out. The results of modeling the operation of a power node, that provides the average daily capacity wind power plant, presented. The results of modeling the optimal use of the power node, which includes wind power plant with a battery electric storage system and a reserve power plant, confirm the possibility of its stable operation as part of the integrated power system of Ukraine.

title: CT-SAFR: Safe and Interpretable Chain-of-Thought Reasoning for Autonomous Robots : A Multi-Layered Verification Framework for Trustworthy AI-Driven Robotic Decision Making
authors: Cagri Temel
abstract: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting enables LLMs to perform explicit, step-by-step reasoning, creating opportunities for sophisticated autonomous robots. However, recent research reveals that reasoning models verbalize their actual decision processes only 25–39% of the time, with faithfulness degrading 44% on complex tasks. This paper presents CT-SAFR (Chain-of-Thought Safety and Faithfulness for Robotics), a multi-layered verification framework achieving 94.2% hallucination detection (n =500, 95% CI: 91.8–95.9%) with sub-500ms latency. Through a warehouse robot case study, this work demonstrates 87% reduction in unsafe reasoning outputs (p < 0.001) and provides recommendations for responsible deployment of reasoning-capable autonomous robots.

title: A Robotic Simulation Environment for Ultrasound Imaging of Soft Tissue
authors: Jintan Zhang, Yixuan Wu, Adnan Munawar, Peter Kazanzides
abstract: Robot-assisted ultrasound imaging promises enhanced precision and consistency for diagnostic applications, but its development is hindered by the absence of integrated simulation platforms capable of modeling the complex interplay between robotic manipulation, soft tissue deformation, and acoustic wave propagation. Existing simulators lack integrated capabilities for both domains. To address this gap, we present a unified software pipeline that integrates Isaac Sim's FEMbased soft body simulation with k-Wave's acoustic simulation to enable physics-based ultrasound imaging of deformable tissues. Our key technical contribution is a novel data processing algorithm that maintains physically-plausible correspondence between deforming FEM meshes and heterogeneous acoustic property distributions through optimized spatial sampling. We validate our approach in simulation using complex digital patient phantoms with a Universal Robots-based ultrasound system, demonstrating physics-based B-mode imaging under 1 cm tissue compression. This work enables robotic ultrasound system prototyping, algorithm development, and synthetic data generation for machine learning.

title: Cyclical Temporal Encoding and Hybrid Deep Ensembles for Multistep Energy Forecasting
authors: Salim Khazem, Houssam Kanso
abstract: Accurate electricity consumption forecasting is essential for demand management and smart grid operations. This paper introduces a unified deep learning framework that integrates cyclical temporal encoding with hybrid LSTM-CNN architectures to enhance multistep energy forecasting. We systematically transform calendar-based attributes using sine-cosine encodings to preserve periodic structure, and evaluate their predictive relevance through correlation analysis. To exploit both long-term seasonal effects and short-term local patterns, we employ an ensemble model composed of an LSTM, a CNN, and a meta-learner of MLP regressors specialized for each forecast horizon. Using a one-year national consumption dataset, we conduct an extensive experimental study including ablation analyses (with/without cyclical encodings and calendar features) and comparisons with established baselines from the literature. Results demonstrate consistent improvements across all seven forecast horizons, with our hybrid model achieving lower RMSE and MAE than individual architectures and prior methods. These findings confirm the benefit of combining cyclical temporal representations with complementary deep learning structures. To our knowledge, this is the first work to jointly evaluate temporal encodings, calendar-based features, and hybrid ensemble architectures within a unified short-term energy forecasting framework. The code is available at https://github.com/salimkhazem/cai2025

title: Research on the Problems of Restoring Heat Supply to the Reoccupied Cities of Donbas
authors: Sokolenko Kostiantyn, Yevhen Rudniev, Sokolenko Valeriy, Vitaliy Shvets
publish: 2026 IEEE 9th International Conference on Energy Smart Systems (ESS)
abstract: The article offers a comprehensive approach to solving the problem of restoring heat supply to the de-occupied cities of the Donbas. The liberation of the occupied cities of Donbas will pose many challenges to the functioning, recovery and sustainable development of the urban economy. As a working hypothesis, we assume the possibility that energy supplies, primarily natural gas, will be cut off in the Donbas region. This will mean a period of shock adaptation. It is determined that the scale of destruction of urban infrastructure will not allow for a quick restoration of heat supply to the most destroyed cities. The problem of defining the concept of restoring heat and power supply systems in the de-occupied cities is studied. The article analyses the problems of sustainable operation of the electricity supply system in cities and towns during the heating season. The most difficult challenges will be the absence of district heating systems, overloading of power grids, and low energy efficiency of buildings. The development of the concept of heat and power supply systems for cities should be based on specific indicators of population density, taking into account the share of solvent households (subscribers). The basic principles at the stage of developing a heating system concept should include energy efficiency, autonomy, diversification of energy sources, use of renewable energy, environmental friendliness, and sustainable development.

title: Hybrid Feature Fusion for Multimodal Biometric Recognition: A Novel CNN-Handcrafted Feature Approach for Face and Palm Datasets
authors: Rajesh Namdeo Patil, Pravin Sahebrao Patil
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Device Intelligence, Computing and Communication Technologies (DICCT)
abstract: Multimodal biometric systems have attracted a high level of attention because of their improved security and reliability over unimodal ones. This paper proposes a novel hybrid feature extraction technique based on the combination of deep convolutional neural network (CNN) features with handcrafted features for multimodal biometric recognition by using face and palm. The proposed framework derives complementary features by two parallel streams for learning hierarchical representations and handcraft feature module containing Local Binary Patterns (LBP), Histogram of Oriented Gradients (HOG), and Scale-Invariant Feature Transform (SIFT) descriptors. Feature-level fusion is carried out by the help of canonical correlation analysis (CCA) and score-level fusion by weighted sum rule. The Features of the hybrid are classified with Support Vector Machine (SVM) using the radial basis function kernel. Experimental evaluation on publicly available datasets (FERET for face and PolyU Palmprint database) shows significant performance improvement and an overall recognition accuracy of 98.7%, equal error rate (EER) of 1.2% and false acceptance rate (FAR) of 0.8%. The proposed system is up to 3.2 - 5.8% accurate than existing techniques, which proves the effectiveness of fusing learned and engineered features for reliable biometric recognition.

title: Estimation of State of Charge for Lithium Ion Batteries using Data Based Deep Learning Approach
authors: Affan Abbasi, Muhammad Khalid
abstract: In recent decades, urbanization and population growth have led to increased carbon emissions, exacerbating climate change and global warming. This trend has compelled the power industry to transition to alternative energy sources due to heightened fossil fuel consumption and decreasing availability. The utilization of renewable sources for power supply is recognized as a pivotal step toward achieving sustainable energy goals. These sources, of the likes of solar and wind, are variable in nature and do not provide constant dispensable power supply, hence the need for battery energy storage systems (BESS). BESS store surplus energy from these intermittent sources and provide this energy as backup supply in the time of need. In BESS, the state of charge (SoC) holds significant importance for optimizing charge and discharge schedules which directly affect the rate of dispatch of power. The SoC of the BESS depends directly or indirectly on factors such as voltage supply, current source, operational temperatures, and time of use. Therefore, it varies throughout the life cycle of the BESS, highlighting the need for accurate prediction. This study proposes a deep feed forward neural network (DNN) based SoC prediction model for a Li-ion battery. The input layer of the DNN consists of five nodes: voltage, current, temperature along with average current and voltage values. The battery is studied under a variety of temperature conditions in a controlled environment, and data is recorded for constant and variable ambient temperatures. The trained DNN model had an RMSE of 0.025% and showed an accuracy of 2.6% for testing phase.

title: Hybrid 3D Asset Retrieval via Contrastive Vision-Language Matching and Structured Prompt Parsing
authors: Ahmad Alkhaldi, Igli Balla, Rahma El Bouazzaoui, Tirthendu Prosad Chakravorty, Hergys Rexha, Sébastien Lafond, Tristan Klempka
abstract: Searching large repositories to retrieve 3D assets for simulation environments can be tedious, especially when dealing with complex metadata. On the other hand, retrieving relevant assets using natural language remains a challenge, as embedding-based approaches often fail to handle prompts with multiple constraints, while metadata-based methods are too rigid for abstract or semantic queries. This paper proposes a hybrid retrieval framework that combines a vision-language model to extract semantic features from asset images with a structured natural-language parser to map user text queries to retrievable attributes. The system employs a dual-path architecture that utilizes contrastive embeddings for semantic matching, while a fine-tuned Phi-3-Mini model extracts explicit attribute-value pairs for metadata filtering. The approach was evaluated on a custom dataset of 500 multi-view 3D assets with structured annotations. Experimental results showed that the hybrid method outperforms standalone retrieval baselines, increasing top retrieval success by more than 55 percent for complex multi-attribute queries. The proposed framework enhances 3D asset retrieval workflows in simulation environments where both accuracy and attribute control are crucial, paving the way for automated environment creation.

title: Dense-Seg: A Ground Segmentation Method for Dense Point Clouds Based on Triangular Node Representation
authors: Eksan Firkat, Tayir Mijit, Pakzada Jilil, Bin Zhu, Enyong Xu, Jihong Zhu, Askar Hamdulla
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: In autonomous driving technology, accurate ground segmentation of LiDAR data is critical for effective path planning and decision-making. While existing state-of-the-art algorithms have demonstrated compatibility with high-resolution LiDAR systems, comprehensive evaluation and optimal processing of dense 128-channel point clouds across diverse, complex terrains remain an open research challenge. These methods often face trade-offs between computational efficiency and precise boundary retention in non-planar environments. To address this pressing issue, this paper presents Dense-Seg, an innovative general-purpose ground segmentation method tailored for high-resolution LiDAR, with exceptional robustness in complex and unstructured off-road environments. The proposed methodology consists of four synergistic stages. First, a Triangular Node Representation (TNR) encodes the 3-D scene into a regular triangular mesh, reducing the search space and enabling real-time processing. Second, Principal Component Analysis (PCA)-based initial ground segmentation is applied within each node to obtain coarse ground and non-ground points via plane fitting. Third, elevation value-based ground optimization utilizes the Elevation Features of Point Clouds within Nodes (EFPN)—height difference and variance—to correct slope-induced misclassifications. Finally, the Geometric Features of Adjacent Nodes (GFAN) are leveraged through conditional filtering, propagating labels to the most linear neighbor to refine boundaries at plane intersections. This integrated approach significantly improves the precision and reliability of ground segmentation, delivering 83.2 % F1-score and 80.1 % mIoU on the 128-channel GOOSE dataset while maintaining 22 ms per-frame runtime on a single Central Processing Unit (CPU)—outperforming existing classical methods and enabling robust perception for high-resolution autonomous driving systems. The source code is available at https://github.com/Eksanf/DenseSeg

title: A Measurement-Driven Framework of SISO FSA-FMCW Radar for High-Accuracy 2-D Localization
authors: Sijie Chen, You Lv, Xiaoying Zheng, Haojun Ge, Xiaonan Hui, Xianmin Zhang, Shilie Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: Frequency-scanned antenna (FSA)-based frequency-modulated continuous-wave (FMCW) radars provide a compact and hardware-efficient solution for 2-D localization by encoding both range and angle information into a single frequency sweep. However, practical FSA-FMCW systems often suffer from limited angular scanning caused by antenna open-stopband (OSB) effects and degraded direction-of-arrival (DOA) estimation accuracy due to the mismatch between realized antenna responses and idealized models. To address these limitations, this article presents a measurement-driven framework for practical single-input single-output (SISO) FSA-FMCW localization. An OSB-suppressed substrate-integrated waveguide (SIW) leaky-wave antenna (LWA) is employed to provide continuous frequency scanning over a wide angular range from −50° to 35° across 8–11 GHz. Based on measured far-field data, the complex equivalent array response is reconstructed under physical dispersion constraints, enabling signal-subspace-projection (SSP)-based DOA estimation that is robust to antenna-response mismatch and practical nonidealities. Outdoor experiments with the implemented SISO FSA-FMCW radar demonstrate high-accuracy 2-D localization, achieving an angular RMSE of 1.3° and a range RMSE of 11 cm. These results verify the effectiveness of the proposed framework for compact radar sensing platforms requiring accurate localization with low RF complexity.

title: Development and characterization of a coaxial six-path, low-inductance, low-jitter spark gap switch with two gas filling modes for high-current pulsed power applications
authors: Yongyong Zhang, Yueming Zhu, Lulu Deng, Xiaobing Zhang, Y. C. Francis Thio, Xiaoming Zheng, Wuhan Yuan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: This paper presents the design, development, and characterization of a novel six-in-one spark gap switch for high-current pulsed power systems. The switch consists of six independently triggered spark gap channels arranged coaxially and operated in parallel, enabling a total discharge current of up to 300 kA with low system inductance. A trigger-electrode electrical triggering mechanism is employed to ensure synchronous operation across all channels. The switch features two gas filling modes (with Argon and Nitrogen), supporting a wide working voltage range from 5 kV to 20 kV. Experimental results demonstrate low jitter (<25 ns), a fast current rise time, and consistent performance across repeated shots. The overall inductance of the switch assembly is below 25 nH, making it suitable for high-density plasma applications such as coaxial plasma guns. This work provides a robust and scalable solution for high-current pulsed systems requiring high current, low inductance, and stable triggering performance.

title: Experimental Characterization of Epoxy-Bonded Ring-Type Ni/PZT/FeGaCu Composites for Displacement Sensing Applications
authors: Mukesh Soni, Sumit, A. Arockiarajan
abstract: Magnetoelectric (ME) composites have attracted significant attention as advanced functional materials for sensing applications. However, their use in displacement sensing remains limited due to low sensitivity, the requirement of an external bias magnetic field, and inefficient interfacial coupling. Therefore, enhancing sensor performance through optimized geometric design and effective material distribution is crucial for achieving high sensitivity, low-bias operation, and reliable practical implementation. In this study, epoxy-bonded ring-type Ni/PZT/FeGaCu ME composites were systematically investigated. First, various geometrical configurations, including single-ring and distributed-ring structures, were fabricated and experimentally characterized. ME measurements revealed that the square Distributed Ring Structure-4 configuration exhibited the highest ME coefficient of 1742 millivolts per centimetre per oersted, demonstrating significant improvement compared to previously reported ME composites. Furthermore, displacement sensing measurements demonstrated that the single-ring configuration achieved a maximum sensitivity of 5.673 μV/μm with excellent linearity (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> > 0.95), representing up to a 94.77% improvement over previously reported ME sensors. Moreover, fatigue characterization revealed that exposure to 105 cycles of a DC magnetic field caused no measurable degradation in performance, confirming the robustness and long-term stability of the proposed ME composites for displacement sensing applications.

title: HardVault: A Hybrid FPGA-Based Ethereum-Bitcoin Cold Wallet
authors: Joel Poncha Lemayian, Ghyslain Gagnon, Kaiwen Zhang, Pascal Giard
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: Cryptographic wallets play a vital role in securing digital assets within blockchain networks by managing private keys that authorize secure transactions. However, side channel analysis (SCA) attacks have become a serious threat, enabling attackers to extract sensitive information by exploiting algorithmic weaknesses in microcontroller-based wallets, resulting in the loss of millions of dollars in digital assets. In hierarchically deterministic (HD) systems, the compromise of a single primary key can endanger all subsequent child keys, while the use of independent keys for each account introduces complexity and challenges in key management. This work presents HardVault, a field programmable gate array (FPGA)-based cryptocurrency wallet that supports both Bitcoin and Ethereum. HardVault introduces the first hardware wallet architecture that implements both non-deterministic (ND) and HD key generation modes directly in hardware, giving users the flexibility to choose either approach based on their security and usability needs. By leveraging constant-time operations and hardware-enforced private-key isolation, the design significantly improves resilience to SCA attacks. In addition, the architecture prioritizes resource efficiency to minimize area usage without compromising security, making it well-suited for compact, portable hardware wallet applications. Implementation on a ZCU104 FPGA shows that HardVault uses only 27% of available look-up tables (LUTs). Compared to the Trezor One cryptocurrency (crypto) wallet, the proposed implementation achieves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$9\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> higher energy efficiency, <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$8\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> lower latency, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$7\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> higher throughput.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Compact, MR-Compatible Origami Continuum Robot with Variable Length
authors: Qiqiang Hu, Jian Tao, Hu Jin, Hangjie Mo, Erbao Dong, Xiaojian Li, Gang Feng, Shiwu Zhang, Dong Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Robotics and Bionics
abstract: Continuum robots have recently gained attention for their great value in medical applications. This paper presents a novel design approach for MR-compatible medical continuum robots with the ability to variable length and controllable motion at compact size and light weight. The robot employs a small-scale origami continuum structure with an outside diameter of 15 mm, is driven by three tendon-shape memory alloy (SMA) actuators, and senses the motion of actuators by a thin linear potentiometer (Thinpot). The robot system is lightweight at 76.7 g and compact, with the proximal sensing and actuation module measuring only 34 mm in diameter and 90 mm in length. It is first demonstrated that the Thinpot is insensitive to magnetic disturbance and MR-compatible and can realize accurate feedback control of the large stroke of SMA actuators, even in a real 1.5T MR environment. Further, the bending angle and tip position of the robot can be controlled based on an inverse kinematic model. The robots equipped with different tools will show versatility in biomedical applications, such as endoscopy and needle insertion.

title: Self-Navigation in Non-Adversarial Crowds: An Invariant Set-Based Approach
authors: J. Veejay Karthik, Leena Vachhani
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: Self-navigation in noncoordinating crowded environments is a challenging task within multiagent systems consisting of nonholonomic robots operating through local sensing. Here, self-navigation refers to complete autonomy without relying on any external systems for navigation. In this setting, our primary objective is the development of a fast, novel, sensor-driven controller that directly computes control commands to enable safe maneuvering while coexisting with other agents. We propose an input-constrained feedback controller for nonholonomic mobile robots and characterize the associated invariant sets. These invariant sets are the key to maintaining stability and safety amidst noncooperating agents. We then introduce a planning strategy that steers the generation of invariant sets toward the agent’s intended target, enabling robots to compute provably safe control inputs without preplanned paths or trajectories when navigating crowded multiagent environments. The practicality of our technique is demonstrated through hardware experiments, and we provide a comparison with existing navigation strategies where each robot makes independent sensing and decisions. The proposed approach is well-suited to scenarios that demand rapid, on-the-fly control computations based solely on real-time perceived safety bounds.

title: Event-Aware Instructed Assistant for Referring Video Segmentation
authors: Jinyu Liu, Henghui Ding, Shuting He, Yu-Gang Jiang
abstract: Existing referring video segmentation methods often treat a video as a single event consisting of multiple images, overlooking the fact that a video typically contains multiple distinct events. Under such a mechanism, the model needs to directly understand all the complex content in the video and text, which can easily lead to confusion and hallucinations. To address this issue, we propose to decompose a video to a set of simple events by learnable Event Query, and understand complex video content in an event-by-event, easy-to-understand manner. This is based on the observation that natural language expressions often divide a video into distinct, text-related segments, each representing a separate event within a compound event. We introduce EVIS, an Event-Aware Video Instructed Segmentation Assistant, which utilizes text-guided Event Queries to partition a video into simple events, extracting event-aware visual-text features to achieve a hierarchical understanding of the video. Additionally, we propose Object-Pixel-Hybrid Learning, which enables the MLLMs to track targets in long-term videos by integrating fine-grained pixel features with prior object queries. Extensive experimental results on 5 public benchmarks demonstrate EVIS's strong performance in addressing the referring video segmentation task. Code and trained models will be publicly released.

title: Deep Learning-Based Drone Heading Estimation Using BiLSTM and Multi-Head Attention: A Comparative Study of Simulation and Real-World Flight Experiments
authors: Ruppikha Sree Shankar, Vatsal Siotia, Rani Oomman Panicker, R Manoj
abstract: Autonomous drone navigation commonly relies on magnetometer-based heading estimation, which becomes unreliable in environments affected by magnetic interference. This paper presents a system-level real-time heading estimation framework that predicts drone orientation directly from inertial measurement unit (IMU) data without relying on a compass The proposed approach integrates Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks with a Multi-Head Attention mechanism to model temporal dependencies in accelerometer and gyroscope signals. The proposed approach is deployed within a Mission Planner–ArduPilot environment, where IMU data is streamed from the flight controller to an external computation module via MAVLink communication. The model processes sequential sensor data in real time and transmits predicted heading values back to the flight controller, enabling compass-independent navigation in a closed-loop configuration. The system is evaluated using both simulation and real-world flight experiments under compass-disabled conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.082° and Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 0.109° in simulation-based experiments, while maintaining an MAE of 0.0932° in real flight tests. The model maintains stable trajectory tracking across multiple flight maneuvers, including hovering, straight flight, and aggressive turns. These results indicate that the proposed framework provides accurate and consistent heading estimation for real-time UAV navigation without reliance on magnetometer inputs, demonstrating its effectiveness in practical compass-denied flight scenarios.

title: Cyber-Physical Power System Dataset for Cyber Security of Digital Substations
authors: Nicola Cibin, Nadine Kabbara, Alfan Presekal, Ioannis Semertzis, Vetrivel Rajkumar, Himanshu Goyel, Peter Palensky, Alexandru Ştefanov
abstract: Cyber attacks targeting Intelligent Electronic Devices (IEDs) in digital substations can disrupt power system operation, causing equipment damage, instability, cascading failures, and even a blackout. Cyber-Physical Power System (CPPS) datasets are critically needed to develop novel methods for the detection and prevention of cyber attacks on digital substations. In this paper, a novel CPPS dataset is proposed for cyber security of digital substations, including real-time power system measurements, i.e., electromagnetic transient three-phase voltages and currents, communication network traffic, and virtual IED resource metrics. Various scenarios are simulated on an IEC 61850-compliant testbed consisting of Real-Time Digital Simulator (RTDS) and physical and virtual IEDs in hardware-in-the-loop configuration. The dataset contains different operating conditions and cyber attack scenarios, i.e., normal operation, single-phase-to-ground fault, network reconnaissance, resource exhaustion, and IEC 61850 Generic Object-Oriented Substation Event (GOOSE) and Sampled Values (SV) injection attacks. This work aims to provide the research community with a comprehensive and high-fidelity dataset to be used for the design and testing of novel methodologies to increase the cyber security of power grids.

title: Application of Partial MDS Codes to Secret Sharing Schemes
authors: Guohui Wang, Yucheng Chen, Chunming Tang
