title: Multi-Zone Short-Term Load Forecasting Using Time Series and Learning-Based Models
authors: Balkrishna, V R Srividhya, Surbhi Agrawal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: Accurate short-term energy consumption forecasting is essential for efficient power system planning and operation. The integration of smart grids, renewable energy sources, and electrification has increased the complexity of demand patterns influenced by temporal and external factors. This paper presents a comparative framework for 7-day ahead energy consumption forecasting across three zones using statistical, machine learning, and deep learning models. SARIMA, Prophet, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Random Forest (RF) are evaluated under a uniform experimental setup. Data preprocessing includes resampling, missing value handling, feature engineering, and normalization. Model performance is assessed using MAE, RMSE, and MAPE. Results show that forecasting accuracy differs across zones due to varying consumption characteristics. LSTM performs well for nonlinear sequential patterns, while RF is effective for irregular lag-based behavior. SARIMA and Prophet remain competitive for strong seasonal trends. The framework supports informed model selection for reliable energy management and grid operation.

title: Scalable Multimodal Localization for Underground Parking Lots Using Distributed Antenna Systems
authors: Yihong Zheng, Zhaoming Lu, Xinghe Chu, Yinzhe Zhou, Ziwen Luo, Zhiqun Hu, Yuhui Guo
abstract: Achieving accurate and flexible localization in global navigation satellite system (GNSS)-denied underground spaces is critical for Internet of Things (IoT)-enabled logistics and personnel operations. To this end, this paper proposes a scalable multimodal localization framework requiring only a single ultra-wideband (UWB) transmitter connected to a distributed antenna system, avoiding the deployment of multiple additional anchors. An adjacency-masked, change-point-aware hidden Markov model (AC-HMM) is developed for region identification using only two-path UWB measurements, which avoids full channel impulse response (CIR) processing and reduces computational complexity. A multi-scale factor-graph maximum a posteriori inference method (MS-FGM) is then proposed for dynamic localization by fusing UWB and magnetic-field residuals with region and motion constraint factors. Multi-scale temporal aggregation is further introduced to mitigate motion-induced fluctuations and improve localization accuracy and stability. Experiments conducted along the roadways of an underground parking lot demonstrate an average region classification accuracy of 96.81% and a mean positioning error of 0.88 m, outperforming existing methods by up to 42.19%.

title: Experimental Investigation of the Impedance Spectroscopy Suitability for Windings Insulation Prognosis under Different Ageing Mechanisms
authors: A. Douvaras, D. Chronopoulos, D. Glykos, Konstantinos N. Gyftakis
abstract: Stator inter-turn faults are among the most critical faults in electrical machines due to their fast evolution into a catastrophic breakdown. This type of fault is especially critical for electric vehicle applications because it may compromise human safety. This fault is directly linked to the reliability of the electric insulating system. In this paper, the authors investigate the impact of accelerated ageing on the dielectric properties of traction motor windings insulation. The aim is to understand if the impedance spectroscopy is reliable for assessing the health of the insulation and lead to appropriate diagnostic outcomes. Different ageing mechanisms have been applied in order to evaluate the impedance spectroscopy's reliability to capture changes in the insulation materials. The results indicate that the impedance spectroscopy should be used with caution and secondary analysis and criteria need to be developed, mainly due to the opposite effects of the resistive and reactive character of the insulation, which leads to a complex behaviour.

title: A Unified Framework for Unbiased Non-Coherent Over-the-Air Computation
authors: Martin Dahl, Zheng Chen, Erik G. Larsson
abstract: Over-the-Air Computation (OAC) enables efficient data aggregation in large-scale distributed systems by exploiting the superposition property of wireless multiple-access channels. In contrast to most existing studies on OAC assuming exact channel state information, we consider non-coherent OAC (NC-OAC) where the channel phase is unknown at the transmitters. A three-step framework for NC-OAC with a mapping between source data and codewords is proposed: 1) Devices encode their data to non-negative codewords; 2) Devices transmit a sequence of symbols with amplitude proportional to their codewords, such that the receiver can estimate the codeword sum. Estimation of the codeword sum is studied under two scenarios of global channel amplitude knowledge: statistical or instantaneous; 3) The estimated codeword sum is decoded to the desired source data sum at the receiver. With the proposed framework, we first study prior work on NC-OAC and map these to the framework. Next, we define and compare the two most commonly (often implicitly) used mappings for NC-OAC: the Affine and the Augmented Affine mappings. Under the constraint of unbiased estimation, we show that with uniformly distributed data and standard channel assumptions, the Augmented Affine mapping exhibits an order of magnitude lower estimation variance than the Affine mapping with both statistical and instantaneous channel knowledge. This result is validated by extensive simulations. Finally, we propose and analyze a new mapping, which demonstrates superior performance over the previous two affine mappings.

title: Joint Resource Allocation and Beamforming Design for STAR-BD-RIS-Assisted LEO Satellite Collaborative Edge Computing
authors: Jiazi Gao, Xintong Qin, Zhengyu Song, Tianwei Hou, Jun Wang, Wenjuan Yu, Xin Sun
abstract: Low earth orbit (LEO) satellite-assisted edge computing is a promising paradigm for providing seamless connectivity to remote areas and enabling reliable services in dense urban scenarios. By establishing virtual line-of-sight (LoS) links and coherently combining reflected signals, the reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) can significantly enhance the channel conditions of satellite-terrestrial communication links. This paper integrates the novel simultaneously transmitting and reflecting beyond-diagonal RIS (STAR-BD-RIS) technology into the LEO satellite collaborative edge computing (LSCEC) system, where each ground user equipment (GUE) can simultaneously offload task bits to multiple satellites via a hybrid non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) scheme with the aid of STAR-BD-RIS. To minimize the weighted sum energy consumption of GUEs and LEO satellites, the transmit power, CPU frequency, offloading strategy, bandwidth allocation, and STAR-BD-RIS beamforming are jointly optimized by an iterative algorithm based on the successive convex approximation (SCA) technique and the penalty dual decomposition (PDD) method. Simulation results illustrate that: 1) the STAR-BD-RIS scheme achieves lower energy consumption than traditional RIS (T-RIS), simultaneously transmitting and reflecting RIS (STAR-RIS) and beyond-diagonal RIS (BD-RIS) schemes in LSCEC systems; 2) the proposed hybrid NOMA demonstrates superior scalability and performance over both NOMA and orthogonal frequency division multiple access (OFDMA); 3) the proposed satellite collaboration scheme significantly reduces the energy consumption compared to the case without collaboration, while exhibiting diminishing energy-saving gains as the number of satellites increases.

title: A Motor Fault Diagnosis Framework with Vibration-Current Multimodal Contrastive Learning for Cross-Device Scenarios
authors: Yuan Zhuang, Deqiang He, Zhenzhen Jin, Jian Miao, Juan Lu, Jinxin Wu, Baofu Qin
abstract: Fault diagnosis in industrial environments is often constrained by the scarcity of labeled data, variability of operating conditions and cross-device deployment. Moreover, relying solely on vibration signals results in limited diagnostic accuracy and robustness for motors under complex working conditions. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel self-supervised motor fault diagnosis framework based on vibration–current multimodal contrastive learning, in which current features are innovatively incorporated into the contrastive learning paradigm. Specifically, a vibration–current multimodal contrastive learning is first developed to enhance discriminative representation learning while improving cross-modal feature consistency, thereby enabling deeper semantic interaction and complementary information fusion between different modalities. Subsequently, to address the adaptive feature encoding of multimodal signals, a dynamically weighted multi-scale residual encoder is constructed, which effectively extracts heterogeneous modal features by adaptively adjusting the importance of multi-scale representations. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed framework is validated through three case studies. Experimental results demonstrate that an average diagnostic accuracy of 97.75% was achieved across all cross-device tasks using only 5% labeled samples. In addition, the proposed model contains 11.89M parameters and requires only 2.58s for fine-tuning, highlighting its robustness, reliability, and strong application potential in label-limited industrial scenarios.

title: Development of autoencoder-guided attention-LSTM models for predicting nocturnal hypoglycemia risk in Type 1 Diabetes
authors: Konstantinos Tziavaras, Maria Athanasiou, Konstantina S. Nikita
abstract: Objective: Nocturnal hypoglycemia (NH) is a major, often undetected risk for individuals with Type 1 Diabetes (T1DM). Current prediction models lack sufficient lead time for proactive, pre-sleep interventions. This study develops a novel, interpretable deep learning approach for predicting NH events up to 12 hours in advance. Methods: We propose a hybrid model that infuses physiological knowledge (simulated glucose absorption, insulin kinetics, and subcellular insulin signaling) into an attention-based Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network. An Autoencoder-based Attention Weight Mapping (A2WM) framework is introduced to trace model attention back to the original 24-hour inputs (CGM, meals, insulin) for interpretability. The model was developed using the OhioT1DM dataset (open-loop pump users) and externally validated on the SMARTDIAB dataset (open-loop pump users). Results: On the OhioT1DM test set, the model achieved an AUC of 0.9387, recall of 0.9333, and specificity of 0.8548. On the external SMARTDIAB cohort, it demonstrated robust generalizability, achieving an AUC of 0.8684, recall of 0.6316, and specificity of 0.9615. Decision Curve Analysis confirmed a high net clinical benefit, and the A2WM interpretations identified clinically relevant temporal features. Conclusion: The proposed physiologically-informed, attention based LSTM model can accurately and interpretably predict 12 hour NH risk from historical data and scheduled basal insulin. Significance: This work provides a clinically valuable tool for proactive NH management, offering an extended lead time that empowers T1DM patients to implement preventive strategies (e.g., pre-sleep meal or insulin adjustments) well before hypoglycemia occurs.

title: Design of BESS-based Synthetic Inertia for Mitigating the Low Inertia Effects in High IBR Power Systems
authors: Arunkumar Santosh Patil
publish: 2026 International Conference on Recent Advancement in Electrical, Computer and Communication Technologies (IECCT)
abstract: In modern power systems, the demand for effective frequency regulation is increasing, stemming from the rapid penetration of inverter-based resources (IBR), which are displacing conventional synchronous generators (SGs) and reducing overall system inertia. The resulting low-inertia conditions make the grid more susceptible to load–generation imbalances, leading to faster frequency deviations, deeper frequency nadirs, and a higher rate of change of frequency (RoCoF), ultimately threatening system stability. To mitigate these challenges, this study evaluates battery energy storage system (BESS)-based synthetic inertia for frequency regulation in a reconfigured IEEE 9-bus system under high solar PV penetration. To provide synthetic inertia support, the BESS is designed based on inertia forecasting of a reduced-order SG using a hybrid long short-term memory (LSTM)-gated recurrent unit (GRU) model. Simulation results reveal that the proposed approach leads to a significant reduction in RoCoF and improves the frequency nadir, thereby enhancing overall system frequency stability.

title: TwinIt: An AI Digital Twin Platform for IoT prototyping using LLM
authors: Aswin V, Armintha Bobby S.R, Sonia Jenifer Rayen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networks, and Security (IC-ICNS)
abstract: Many students and developers face difficulties in transforming Internet of Things (IoT) ideas into working prototypes but due to limited access to hardware , dependence on development tools, and the programming skills required to work with the hardware. These challenges slow down the early experimentation and restrict hands - on learning. Although existing IoT simulation and digital twin tools provides prototyping service, they do not involve in automatic generations of functional and validated IoT simulations. To address this gap, this work presents TwinIt , an LLM - driven platform that automatically generates digital twins of IoT systems from simple natural - language descriptions. TwinIt enables rapid, hardware-free IoT prototyping by interpreting user intent, selecting appropriate virtual components, constructing the system architecture , and simulating real - time behaviour of the digital twin prototype. The key novelty of this framework is its integrated dual - layer validation mechanism, which verifies both the structural correctioness of the generated IoT model and the accuracy its real - time behaviour during simulation. This validation provides immediate and meaningful feedback to users, improving reliability and learning outcomes. TwinIt works to lower technical barriers and eliminating the need for physical devices, through supporting faster concept exploration, enhancing IoT education among students, and enabling early-stage system testing for both beginners and professionals in academic and industrial environments.

title: 2-D Electric Field Prediction Using Neural Networks Trained on Mobile Sensor Network Observations
authors: Ran Li, Zhaoting Liu, Ying Huang, Shuocheng Wang, Qinglin Xia
abstract: Accurate prediction of the two-dimensional electromagnetic field strength (EMFS) is critical for practical applications such as wireless network planning and electromagnetic compatibility assessment. This paper focuses on predicting the EMFS distribution from a limited set of measurements acquired by a network of mobile sensors. To address this, we present an integrated framework combining adaptive data collection with optimized machine learning reconstruction. In the data acquisition phase, an adaptive control strategy, inspired by bacterial chemotaxis, guides mobile sensor nodes to effectively explore and track the EMFS radiated by an antenna. To reconstruct the EMFS spatial distribution, we employ data-driven approaches, such as multilayer perceptrons (MLP) and radial basis function neural networks (RBFNN), combined with the grey wolves optimization (GWO) algorithm. Unlike traditional interpolation methods that rely on predefined assumptions, neural network models, MLP and RBFNN learn spatial patterns from data, offering enhanced generalization and adaptability. Experimental results demonstrate the practical superiority of this integrated approach: the proposed RBFNN-GWO method achieves a normalized reconstruction error (NRE) of 0.007 ± 0.001 in free-space scenarios. Furthermore, in more realistic environments with building obstructions, the GWO-based approach maintains a stable mean NRE of 0.149 , validating the system’s robust performance in complex scenarios with limited and nonuniform data.

title: 2026 IEEE Swiss Conference on Data Science and AI (SDS)
authors: N/A

title: Robust Optimization Techniques and Their Application in the Design of Coherent Filterless Optical Networks
authors: Mohammad M. Hosseini, João Pedro, Antonio Napoli
abstract: This article investigates robust design methodologies for cost-effective and resilient design of filterless Point-to-Multipoint (P2MP) optical networks by minimizing the number of amplifiers, which are often compromised by parameter uncertainties, for example inaccurate fiber loss. To overcome the inefficiencies of traditional, overly or insufficiently conservative margin-based designs, this study investigates Deterministic Optimization (DO), Robust Optimization (RO), and Adjustable Robust Optimization (ARO) of optical filterless horseshoe networks. All these techniques are explained in detail using a simple Linear Program (LP) model example. The findings of this manuscript demonstrate that ARO is a significantly superior technique, leveraging a two-stage, “wait-and-see” approach that allows for adaptive adjustments, by modifying amplifier gains after uncertainty is revealed. This adaptability reduces the conservatism of standard RO, achieving full resiliency with a much lower “price of robustness”. Specifically, for the considered scenarios, at a 20% uncertainty level, ARO required only a 16% increase in amplifiers compared to 40% for RO, ultimately providing a more efficient trade-off between performance and robustness.

title: A Comparative Study on Current Spreading Layers in n-AlGaN to Enhance the Quantum Efficiency of Deep Ultraviolet Light-Emitting Diodes_supp1-3696595.docx
authors: Wei Guo
abstract: In AlGaN-based deep ultraviolet light-emitting diodes (DUV-LEDs), the significant imbalance in carrier concentration between electrons and holes induces a severe electron crowding effect and current overshoot, ultimately limiting the external quantum efficiency (EQE). To address this issue, three types of current-spreading layers (CSLs)—namely, a superlattice, a high-Al-content insertion layer, and a modulation-doping layer—were respectively introduced between the multiple quantum wells (MQWs) and the n-AlGaN layer. The corresponding band diagrams and carrier distributions were investigated through technology computer-aided design (TCAD) simulations. Experimental results further demonstrate that the modulation doping structure achieves the most effective current spreading, substantially mitigating sidewall current crowding. This configuration yields a peak EQE exceeding 9% and the lowest junction temperature among all designs. This work presents a deeper understanding of the development of high-performance DUV light emitters.

title: Subject-Adaptive EEG-EMG Fusion Network for Motor Intention Decoding Across a Broad Impairment Spectrum
authors: Yihan Wang, Jiaxing Wang, Weiqun Wang, Jianqiang Su, Bin Hu, Yuze Feng, Shuai Han, Hao Li, Jianghong He, Zeng-Guang Hou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The fusion of electroencephalography (EEG) and electromyography (EMG) holds significant potential for clinical motor intention decoding. However, existing methods are constrained by the dual challenges of data scarcity and high physiological heterogeneity across patient populations. To address these limitations, this paper proposes the Brain-Muscle based Motor Planning to Execution Network (BM-MP2E), a framework leveraging brain-muscle complementarity to ensure reliable decoding across the full impairment spectrum. We introduce a physiologically-informed architecture that explicitly mirrors the neuromuscular transmission pathway. Specifically, movement-related cortical potential (MRCP) for motor planning, αβ bands for activation, and EMG for execution are extracted to constrain the model’s solution space. Furthermore, a subject-adaptive fusion module is designed to dynamically modulate modality contributions based on individual functional status. Experimental validation on 21 patients (ranging from Disorders of Consciousness to stroke) in a unimanual five-class task demonstrates that BM-MP2E achieves an average accuracy of 60.81%, yielding a 6.94% improvement over single-modality EMG. The average learned weights were 0.265 (MRCP), 0.323 (αβ), and 0.412 (EMG). Quantitative analysis reveals a strong positive correlation between adaptive EMG weights and motor function scores (r =0.884, p < 0.0001). This confirms a compensatory measurement logic: the system automatically prioritizes stable cortical features in severe cases while progressively leveraging high-fidelity peripheral signals as function recovers. These findings validate BM-MP2E as an adaptive clinical solution capable of facilitating motor assistance across the entire recovery spectrum.

title: Reliable Covert Communication for Integrated Cognitive Satellite Aerial Terrestrial Networks with NOMA and Poisson Distributed Jammers
authors: Kefeng Guo, Peilin Qi, Shahid Mumtaz, Yuzhen Huang, Ali Nauman, Lei Zhang, Qihui Wu
abstract: With the increasing threats of eavesdropping in wireless networks, covert transmission has become a growing area of concerns. To address this challenge, this paper investigates the reliability of covert communication (CC) in integrated satellite aerial terrestrial networks with homogeneous Poisson point process (HPPP) distributed jammers. In addition, non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) and cognitive radio (CR) techniques are employed to enhance spectrum efficiency. To assess the covert performance of the system under consideration, closed-form expressions for the detection error probability (DEP), covert outage probability (COP), and covert transmission rate (CTR) are derived. Moreover, an optimization method is also provided to achieve the optimal COP by adjusting the power allocation factors under power constraints. Furthermore, Monte Carlo (MC) simulation results are conducted to verify the accuracy of the theoretical results. It is revealed that, compared to constant or uniformly distributed interference power, the ability of the considered system to maintain covert operations is significantly enhanced by the presence of Poisson distributed jammers, which indicates the importance of considering realistic, random interference models in CC systems and offers valuable insights for improving system reliability and security.

title: Distributed Semantic Communication via Nonlinear Transform Coding for Correlated Images
authors: Yufei Bo, Meixia Tao, Kai Niu
abstract: This paper investigates distributed source-channel coding for correlated image semantic communication, where different but correlated image sources from distributed transmitters are separately encoded and transmitted through dedicated wireless channels to a common receiver. We propose a novel distributed nonlinear transform coding (NTC)-based approach to explicitly model source correlation from both probabilistic and geometric perspectives. The probabilistic perspective is achieved by a joint entropy model that approximates the joint distribution of latent representations and guides adaptive rate allocation, while the geometric perspective is achieved by a feature alignment module that aligns latent features for maximal correlation learning at the decoder.We implement this distributed NTC framework for both source coding only, referred to as D-NTSC, and joint source-channel coding (JSCC), referred to as D-NTSCC. Variational inference is employed to derive principled loss functions that jointly optimize encoding, decoding, and joint entropy modeling. Extensive experiments on real-world multi-view datasets demonstrate that D-NTSC and D-NTSCC outperform existing distributed source coding and distributed JSCC baselines, respectively, achieving state-of-the-art performance in both pixel-level and perceptual quality metrics.

title: Position-Aware Hybrid Beamforming for ISAC: Leveraging RIS and Stacked Intelligent Metasurfaces
authors: Liming Wang, Nan Wu, Rongkun Jiang, Jiayin Zhang, Mehul Motani, Arumugam Nallanathan
abstract: Although the stacked intelligent metasurface (SIM) has shown promise in enhancing integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) systems, its performance is fundamentally limited by blockages in practical scenarios. A natural solution is to introduce a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) to alleviate this blockage problem. However, deploying the RIS brings new challenges, such as the “double fading” effect, which can severely degrade the end-to-end signal strength. To overcome these challenges, this paper investigates a novel ISAC framework that leverages SIM in conjunction with RIS to enable position-aware hybrid beamforming. We first derive the Cramér-Rao bound (CRB) for estimating the two-dimensional direction-of-arrival of the sensing target. Based on the proposed framework, the phase shifts of SIM and RIS, as well as the deployment location of the RIS, are jointly optimized to maximize the sum rate of users subject to a CRB constraint. However, this CRB constraint introduces a highly complex non-convexity with respect to the optimization variables, making direct optimization intractable. We therefore derive an intuitive relationship between the CRB and beampattern gain to enable tractable optimization. To tackle the resulting non-convex optimization problem, two efficient algorithms are developed: a high-performance algorithm termed SGE (based on successive convex approximation and gradient ascent with element-wise coordinate ascent), and a low-complexity method based on double gradient ascent with successive convex approximation (DGAS). Extensive simulation results demonstrate that introducing the RIS delivers a 1.5 times higher sum rate subject to the sensing constraint in challenging propagation conditions. Furthermore, RIS location optimization confers an additional gain relative to its fixed position counterpart.

title: MPDA: Multimodal Prompt Decoupling Attack on the Safety Filters in Text-to-Image Models
authors: Xingkai Peng, Jun Jiang, Meng Tong, Shuai Li, Weiming Zhang, Nenghai Yu, Kejiang Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Text-to-image (T2I) models have been widely applied in generating high-fidelity images across various domains. However, these models may also be abused to produce Not-Safe-for-Work (NSFW) content via jailbreak attacks. Existing jailbreak methods primarily manipulate the textual prompt, leaving potential vulnerabilities in image-based inputs largely unexplored. Moreover, text-based methods face challenges in bypassing the model's safety filters. In response to these limitations, we propose the Multimodal Prompt Decoupling Attack (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathsf {MPDA}$</tex-math></inline-formula>), which utilizes image modality to separate the harmful semantic components of the original unsafe prompt. <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathsf {MPDA}$</tex-math></inline-formula> follows three core steps: firstly, a large language model (LLM) decouples unsafe prompts into pseudo-safe prompts and harmful prompts. The former are seemingly harmless sub-prompts that can bypass filters, while the latter are sub-prompts with unsafe semantics that trigger filters. Subsequently, the LLM rewrites the harmful prompts into natural adversarial prompts to bypass safety filters, which guide the T2I model to modify the base image into an NSFW output. Finally, to ensure semantic consistency between the generated NSFW images and the original unsafe prompts, the visual language model generates image captions, providing a new pathway to guide the LLM in iterative rewriting and refining the generated content. Extensive experiments empirically demonstrate that <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathsf {MPDA}$</tex-math></inline-formula> outperforms the baseline methods in two attack scenarios across Stable Diffusion 3.5 and three commercial models: CogView, Tongyiwanxiang, and Midjourney. Specifically, in the pornographic content attack scenario with Midjourney as the victim model, our method has a 29% higher bypass rate than the previous method, thereby providing robust empirical evidence supporting the effectiveness and generalizability of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathsf {MPDA}$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Disclaimer: This paper contains content that readers may find offensive, distressing, or upsetting. Reader discretion is advised.

title: Optimized Algorithms for FPGA-Based Acceleration of 5G-NR PDSCH Transmit Chain
authors: Samudrala Soujanya, Harsha Rudramuniyappa, Prem Singh, Ekant Sharma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: This paper presents optimized algorithms and hardware architecture for field-programmable gate array (FPGA)-based acceleration of the fifth-generation (5G) new radio (NR) physical downlink shared channel (PDSCH). The paper proposes a modular multi-intellectual property (IP) architecture that supports variable data widths across different processing blocks to efficiently accommodate the wide dynamic range of transport block sizes and various modulation schemes. The proposed implementation on a system-on-chip (SoC) based custom board achieves a significant reduction in latency, generating the first set of modulated symbols within <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$32 \mu s$</tex-math> </inline-formula> for a subcarrier spacing (SCS) of 30KHz, within the duration of a single symbol of 5G frame. The complete chain delivers a throughput of 167 Mbps, power efficiency of 698.74 Mbps/W, energy consumption of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.39~m\mathrm {J}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, and resource utilization of only 4.05% of the total programmable logic (PL) resources of target device, while maintaining a latency to meet 5G-NR frame boundaries, even for the maximum bandwidth allocation for PDSCH in frequency range 1 (FR1) using 64-quadrature amplitude modulation (QAM) modulation. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed FPGA-based architecture for scalable, low-latency, and energy-efficient PDSCH processing in real-time 5G physical layer deployments.

title: Policy-Driven Shift-Left Security for Hybrid OpenShift CI/CD Pipelines
authors: Nilesh Jaiswal
publish: 2026 International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Systems, and Emerging Technologies (ICAISET)
abstract: Enterprise adoption of hybrid cloud platforms such as Red Hat OpenShift has increased the complexity of securing containerized workloads across heterogeneous infrastructure. Traditional CI/CD pipelines defer vulnerability detection and policy validation to late stages, allowing insecure artifacts to propagate toward production. This paper presents a policy-driven shift-left security framework that integrates three controls into the pre-deployment stage of hybrid OpenShift CI/CD pipelines: container image vulnerability scanning using Trivy, Kubernetes deployment policy enforcement using Open Policy Agent (OPA) with eight Rego-based denial rules, and governance tag validation against a four-label compliance schema. We evaluate the framework through a controlled experiment comparing a baseline pipeline (no security checks) against the shift-left pipeline across 50 builds deployed to a Kubernetes namespace, comprising 35 compliant, 8 vulnerable, 5 policy-violating, and 2 governance-noncompliant configurations. The baseline pipeline deploys all 50 builds without checks, while the shift-left pipeline blocks all 15 insecure builds (100% detection rate) with zero false positives. The shift-left pipeline introduces a mean overhead of 28.3 seconds per build, with container image scanning accounting for 82.2% of the added time. Results demonstrate that early-stage integration of layered security controls eliminates insecure deployments in hybrid OpenShift environments without disrupting compliant workloads.

title: Solution of High Aspect Ratio TSV PVD in Advanced Packaging
authors: Tongwen Zhang, Lucinda Wang
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: With the Moore’s law is approaching physical limit, and the big data and artificial intelligence rapidly developing, more and more attention is focused on the advanced packaging. The TSV interconnect is one of the most important process in the advanced packaging. Cost down and performance improvement of TSV tool is very critical and important to the advanced packaging industry.AMEC has developed Primo InnoFill PVD system targeting at high aspect ratio TSV for the advanced packaging. The PVD system integrates several unique technology, including low cost and high throughput dual stations design, unique side electromagnet assembly design, adding high frequency RF to DC power to target and so on. These unique designs of the Primo InnoFill PVD system deliver good RS uniformity, high step coverage with significantly lower cost. In this paper, we introduce the details of AMEC Primo InnoFill PVD design and the performance.

title: Hypernetwork-Mediated Multimodal Fusion for Breast Cancer Molecular Subtype Classification Using Histopathology and Genomics
authors: Faseela Abdullakutty, Younes Akbari, Somaya Al-Maadeed, Ahmed Bouridane, Rifat Hamoudi, Suchithra Kunhoth
publish: 2026 IEEE 5th International Conference on Computing and Machine Intelligence (ICMI)
abstract: Accurate molecular subtyping of breast cancer into luminal versus basal/HER2 subtypes remains difficult due to limited integration of histopathology and genomic data. Existing multimodal fusion strategies often underexploit cross-modal interactions and tend to overfit small cohorts, resulting in suboptimal classification. To address this, we propose a hypernetwork-based fusion framework where gene expression profiles dynamically modulate CNN parameters for histopathology analysis. Results on 5-fold cross-validation show that unimodal genomic models perform strongly, whereas image-only models perform poorly, and conventional early fusion provides no significant improvement. In contrast, the proposed hypernetwork-mediated fusion achieves 0.96 accuracy, with consistently high F1-score and AUC, clearly outperforming both unimodal and standard multimodal approaches on the same cohort. This establishes hypernetwork fusion with cross-modal augmentation as an effective and generalizable strategy for robust breast cancer subtyping, offering potential for precision oncology applications.

title: Generation of Frequency-independent and Phase-coherent Dual-band Microwave Waveforms Based on Mutual Injection Locked OEO
authors: Mengyuan Guan, Huiyun Tang, Shiyu Xiao, Nan Zhang, Jianing Jiang, Ming Li, Wei Li
abstract: We propose and experimentally demonstrate a photonic-assisted scheme to generate frequency independent and phase-coherent dual-band microwave waveforms with reconfigurable modulation formats based on a mutual injection locked optoelectronic oscillator (OEO). The proposed system is implemented using a dual polarization Mach–Zehnder modulator (DPol-MZM). One polarization branch is used to construct the OEO loop and generate frequency-independent and phase-coherent dual band microwave signals through intracavity mutual injection locking. The other orthogonal polarization branch is driven by electrical baseband coding signals. After polarization recombination, dual-band microwave waveforms with programmable modulation formats are produced by beating in a photodetector. In the experiments, the proposed system is capable of generating phase coherent dual-band microwave signals in the C and X bands simultaneously, with phase noise of approximately −123 dBc/Hz at 10 kHz. The generated signals can be flexibly reconfigured between phase-coded microwave pulses at 1 Gb/s and complementary linearly chirped microwave waveforms (LCMWs) with a bandwidth of 2 GHz. The proposed scheme provides a promising platform for dual band cooperative sensing, high-precision information processing, and improved spectral efficiency in radar and communication systems.

title: Innovative Solution for Ta Pillar Metal Hard-Mask Open in MRAM Technology
authors: Lin Shao, Chao Dong, Jian Huang, Caigan Chen, Nian Han
publish: 2026 Conference of Science and technology of Integrated Circuits (CSTIC)
abstract: Tantalum (Ta) is widely utilized as a metal hard-mask (MHM) for etching magnetic tunneling junction (MTJ) multilayer structures in MRAM manufacturing. The profile and critical dimension (CD) uniformity of Ta MHM are crucial for precise MTJ CD control. However, Ta MHM plasma etching presents several challenges, including achieving a vertical Ta profile, removing non-volatile metal etching by-products, and maintaining precise CD uniformity. This paper proposes innovative solutions to address these challenges. By incorporating F-based chemistry into Cl<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> chemistry, more volatile by-products, TaF<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf>, are formed instead of TaCl<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf> alone, resulting in highly vertical Ta profiles. Additionally, the use of Cl-based flush effectively removes metal etching by-products without damaging dielectric HM. Furthermore, excellent CD uniformity is achieved through the application of Metal NXP hydra ESC. These advancements significantly enhance Ta MHM plasma etching processes, thereby improving MTJ CD control and MRAM manufacturing efficiency.

title: A SINS/OFSS Integrated Positioning Method for Enhancing Shearer Positioning Accuracy in Intelligent Coal Mining Face
authors: Haotian Feng, Xinqiu Fang, Gang Wu, Ningning Chen, Yang Song, Dexing He, Junyue Fan, Minfu Liang, Lijun Sun
abstract: In the context of intelligent coal mining faces, the strapdown inertial navigation system (SINS) serves as a pivotal technology for realizing the autonomous positioning of shearers. However, due to the complex underground environment, the positioning precision and stability of SINS have yet to meet the requirements for the normalized operation of intelligent mining faces. This limitation severely impedes the widespread application of SINS in coal mines. To address these challenges and enhance the performance of shearer SINS, this paper proposes a positioning accuracy improvement method based on the SINS and optical fiber sensing system (OFSS). By leveraging the spatial consistency between the shearer trajectory and the shape curve of the scraper conveyor, a SINS/OFSS integrated positioning model tailored to the shearer’s motion characteristics is established. Furthermore, a position mapping mechanism is introduced to achieve spatiotemporal consistency between the real-time position of the shearer and the discrete points on the scraper conveyor’s shape curve. Subsequently, a shearer trajectory time-series prediction model is constructed using historical states to predict future trajectories. This model serves to evaluate and perform a secondary correction on the positioning results, thereby further strengthening the error correction capability. Finally, simulation experiments and platform tests are conducted to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method. The results indicate that under both normal cutting and oblique cutting conditions, the positioning precision and stability of the proposed method significantly outperform comparative methods. The combination of the SINS/OFSS integrated positioning model and the TCN-BiLSTM-Attention (TBA) prediction model effectively mitigates SINS positioning errors, realizing a substantial improvement in shearer positioning accuracy.

title: An Industrially Scalable Embroidered Circularly Polarized Antenna with Reduced SAR for Wearable Applications
authors: Zimin Cai, Zhensheng Chen, Qixiang Wang, Weiwei Zhao, Gaojie Chen, Chunxu Mao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: An industrially scalable, single-layer, embroidered circularly polarized (CP) textile antenna with a reduced profile and specific absorption rate (SAR) is proposed for 2.4 GHz ISM-band applications. The antenna is simply composed of a slotted substrate integrated cavity (SIC), featuring a simple configuration and low profile. The CP radiation is achieved by introducing a pair of ±45° asymmetrical crossed slots and a shorting pin. In addition, an auxiliary slot is etched near the probe feed to suppress the out-of-band radiation. Moreover, the antenna exhibits a super stability in terms of impedance/AR bandwidth, gain and radiation pattern under different conformal scenarios. The antenna is fabricated on a real industrial embroidery production line, enabling high repeatability, low production cost, and seamless compatibility with mass-manufacturing workflows. Thanks to the cavity-slot structure, the proposed antenna exhibits very low SAR values of 0.114 W/kg and 0.089 W/kg for 1-g and 10-g averaging when evaluated on a human phantom, demonstrating its suitability for safe and practical wearable deployment.

title: Polarforming for IRS-Assisted Flexible Intelligent Metasurfaces Enabled Wireless Communications
authors: Saba Mehrzad, Kamran Ebrahimi, Ali Attaran, Fang Fang
abstract: In this paper, we propose a novel polarforming design for a flexible intelligent metasurface (FIM)-enabled system assisted by a polarized IRS (P-IRS). Polarforming has emerged as a transformative technique for dynamically shaping antenna polarization to align with electromagnetic (EM) waves, thereby unlocking polarization degrees of freedom (DoFs) to enhance wireless communication performance. The proposed architecture utilizes a polarforming antenna (PA) FIM (P-FIM) at the base station (BS), capable of reconfiguring both surface morphology and polarization states to exploit spatial and polarization DoFs in a MISO setup. Complementing this, a P-IRS is deployed to facilitate polarization-aware wavefront control to further enhance spatial diversity and signal reception. We formulate a joint optimization problem involving the BS beamforming vector, P-FIM morphing, polarforming vectors, and P-IRS reflection coefficients to maximize system sum-rate. To address the challenges of dynamic channel conditions, we develop a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)-enhanced Meta Soft Actor-Critic (Meta-SAC) algorithm. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed scheme in combating channel depolarization and demonstrate its superior performance gain over conventional systems.

title: Biaxial Optical Cantilever Combining on-Chip Patterned Laser and Sampling Moiré Method
authors: Soya Sato, Toshihiro Shiratori, Kazuyoshi Hirose, Hiroki Kumon, Hiroki Kamei, Shu Honma, Yuki Okamoto, Yusuke Takei, Hidetoshi Takahashi
abstract: The optical lever method is a widely used high-resolution displacement sensing technique in various micro- and nanoscale sensing systems. In this method, cantilever displacement is evaluated by detecting changes in the position of reflected light using a photodiode; however, precise optical alignment is required during setup. To address this issue, optical lever systems combining a cantilever with the sampling moiré (SM) method have been proposed. More recently, approaches using on-chip pattern lasers that directly project periodic patterns have attracted attention, as they simplify both system configuration and cantilever fabrication. In many applications, not only normal displacement but also torsional deformation must be measured to evaluate multi-axis forces and moments. Although the SM method is well suited for analyzing two-dimensional periodic patterns, generating well-defined two-dimensional lattice patterns using mechanical gratings is technically challenging. In this study, we propose a biaxial optical cantilever sensor that integrates the SM method with an on-chip pattern laser capable of directly projecting a two-dimensional checkerboard pattern. The projected pattern was captured using a lens-less camera and analyzed using the SM method. Experimental results confirmed that the checkerboard pattern shifted independently in two directions depending on the force application point. The achieved resolutions were 32.4 nN for the normal force Fz and 201 μN·μm for the bending moment Mx, demonstrating the feasibility of simple and high-resolution two-axis force measurement.

title: G-DRAGON: Geospatial Reasoning and Dynamic Planning for Retrieval-Augmented Outdoor Navigation
authors: Dongzhihan Wang, Yi Du, Jianan Sun, Yuan Xue, Yingchen Zhang, Bing Xiao, Chen Wang, Liang Xu
abstract: Autonomous ground robots operating in large-scale outdoor environments require both robust long-range navigation and fine-grained “last-mile” exploration. Current advances in visual-language navigation (VLN) work well at short-range tasks, lacking geospatial grounding for long-distance missions. Some OpenStreetMap (OSM)-based methods relying on cloud-based Large Language Models (LLMs) are prone to factual hallucination and cannot conduct “last-mile” exploration based on human instruction. To address these challenges, we present G-DRAGON, a retrieval-augmented framework for outdoor, open-world navigation. This framework maps natural-language commands to versioned, local OSM entities via generative retrieval based on lightweight LLM, yielding accurate coordinates for global route planning. A high-level planning module bridges global topological routes with the SLAM system, projecting geospatial waypoints into the robot's navigable frame. For the “last mile,” the framework transitions to frontier-based exploration and open-set semantic voxel mapping to localize open-vocabulary targets. Experimental results in simulation demonstrate our framework outperforms state-of-the-art baselines. Furthermore, we validate the system in unseen real-world urban environments on an Unmanned Ground Vehicle (UGV), successfully completing person-search missions with trajectories of up to 500m.

title: Characterization and Initial Optimization of Reconstruction Performance for Detector Crosstalk of Ultra-high Performance Brain PET Imager
authors: Jiazhen Zhang, Kathryn Fontaine, Tiantian Li, Paul Gravel, Takuya Toyonaga, Yifan Hu, Jean-Dominique Gallezot, Tianyi Zeng, Tommaso Volpi, Yihuan Lu, Thibault Marin, Jinyi Qi, Richard E. Carson
publish: IEEE Transactions on Radiation and Plasma Medical Sciences
abstract: The NeuroEXPLORER (NX) is a next-generation, ultra-high-performance brain PET/CT system designed for both high spatial resolution and sensitivity. To meet high-sensitivity requirements, the system uses 2-cm-long scintillation crystals, while the novel U-shaped depth-of-interaction (DOI) encoding and small crystal elements enable high resolution. However, using long, small crystals increases signal crosstalk between crystals arising from Compton scattering, photoelectron escape, and incomplete optical isolation, which can degrade spatial resolution. Although the NX electronics detect crosstalk between micro-blocks and support real-time recovery using measured energy, accurate identification of the first interaction point is not always possible. Incorporating crosstalk events increases coincidence sensitivity by approximately 50% but compromises spatial resolution. Given such a large fraction of detector crosstalk, evaluating its impact on reconstruction performance in the NX is imperative. In this work, we evaluated image resolution in three reconstruction modes: high-sensitivity (HS; all events), higher-resolution (HR; non-crosstalk events), and lower-resolution (LR; crosstalk events), using real phantoms and human studies with multiple tracers. Guided by the resolution characterization of the HYDRA-OLG brain phantom, we introduced a two-Gaussian mixture point spread function (2G-PSF) for HS reconstruction to improve contrast while suppressing noise. At matched iterations, HR yielded the highest image contrast, HS showed slightly reduced contrast, and LR exhibited the largest partial volume effect, as expected. Modeling crosstalk with 2G-PSF for HS increased contrast toward HR levels without sacrificing sensitivity. Overall, these results provide a practical strategy for including crosstalk events in high-resolution PET systems while mitigating their impact on spatial resolution.

title: Coupling-Aware Edge-Cloud Computing for Multi-UAV Cooperative Sensing via Evolutionary Game-Based Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yishan Chen, Tong Zhou, Qing Xu, Guanjie Cheng
abstract: For multi-UAV cooperative perception applications in Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS), edge-cloud collaborative computing is severely constrained by co-channel interference and limited edge computing resources, which induce strong network-level coupling among UAV offloading decisions. In distributed settings where global information is unavailable, this coupling exacerbates environmental non-stationarity during multi-agent training, leading to slow convergence and policy oscillations. To address these issues, we propose a distributed decision-making framework based on evolutionary game theory (EGT). Specifically, we first formulate the multi-UAV task offloading problem as an evolutionary game, capturing the payoff interdependence caused by wireless interference and edge resource contention. Subsequently, we apply replicator dynamics to analyze the evolution of offloading strategies, derive the interior evolutionary equilibrium, and establish its asymptotic stability. Finally, we develop an EGT-enhanced multi-agent deep reinforcement learning (EGT-MADQN) algorithm, which incorporates relative fitness derived from coupling-affected payoffs into strategy fusion through adaptive step-size modulation to to facilitate stable policy adaptation during training. Extensive simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms baseline approaches in minimizing total cost and task latency while achieving superior convergence stability. It also exhibits strong robustness and scalability across varying UAV fleet sizes and resource configurations.

title: Communication-Pipelined Split Federated Learning for Foundation Model Fine-Tuning in UAV Networks
authors: Zizhen Zhou, Ying-Chang Liang, Yanyu Cheng, Wei Yang Bryan Lim
abstract: Deploying foundation models (FMs) on uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs) promises broad “low-altitude economy” applications. Split federated learning (SFL)-based fine-tuning leverages distributed data while keeping raw data local and reduces client-side burden by partitioning the model between client and server. However, the per-round training latency is dominated by stragglers. Training paradigms featuring parallel gradient transmission (GT) allocate dedicated portions of downlink communication resources to each client. They may leave resources idle and suffer from prolonged GT latency, especially in UAV networks, where the communication latency typically far exceeds the computation latency. To address this, we propose a sequential GT paradigm, where the server dedicates all downlink resources for the current GT. We further propose communication-pipelined SFL (CPSFL), characterized by downlink GT priority scheduling and intra-round asynchronous training. We investigate CPSFL-based LoRA fine-tuning of FMs in UAV networks and formulate an optimization problem to minimize a weighted sum of per-round training latency and worst-case client energy consumption by optimizing the split point selection (SPS) and the computing and communication resource allocation (CCRA) (the uplink bandwidth allocation and the server computing frequency allocation). To solve this problem, we develop an attention-based deep reinforcement learning (DRL) framework, where the base station agent decides the split point and the CCRA in each round by leveraging previous round information, including UAV trajectories. Simulation results show that the proposed DRL-based CPSFL scheme outperforms the parallel GT baselines, the ablation variants, and the heuristic CCRA scheme while approaching the best fixed-SPS scheme.

title: Narrow Linewidth Butterfly Laser with Dual-Zone Thermally Symmetric TEC for FMCW LiDAR
authors: Liang Zhang, Xiaochuan Xia, Haoran Ma, Yang Liu, Hongwei Liang
abstract: This paper designs a dual-zone thermally symmetric thermoelectric control (TEC) architecture to improve the thermal and power stability of narrow-linewidth laser modules for frequency-modulated continuous-wave (FMCW) light detection and ranging (LiDAR). Two independently regulated TEC zones with mirrored heat paths are employed to balance heat flow between the laser-diode (LD) and fiber-coupling regions. Finite-element simulations and experiments indicate that the internal temperature difference decreases from approximately 3.5°C to below 2°C. As a result, an output power of up to 67.6 mW at the current 250 mA can be achieved, while the linewidth is 81.5 kHz, the relative intensity noise is less than -140 dB/Hz over 0 Hz–1000 MHz, and the power variation is reduced to ±0.2 dB from −40°C to +85°C. The results demonstrate the effective suppression of stress-induced optical noise and frequency drift via thermal symmetry. The constructed architecture provides a compact, manufacturable, and temperature-resilient solution for high-coherence laser modules in advanced FMCW LiDAR and relevant photonic applications.
